| Bondi is covering up the indiscriminate murder of American citizens by ICE and the identities of the wealthy elite who committed heinous acts of pedophilia and violent sexual abuse alongside Epstein.
Bondi herself displayed the arrogance of someone who does not believe she will ever face accountability.
And that's because accountability is not part of MAGA's plan.
It was Bondi's FBI that seized election records in Georgia. It is Bondi who attempted to extort voter records from Minnesota. It was Bondi's FBI who summoned state election officials to a secret meeting last week.
Pam Bondi is central to the MAGA plan to steal elections, and she knows that if they succeed, none of them will EVER be held to account. That is why Defend Our Courts exists.
If federal leaders refuse to enforce the rule of law, state courts, and ultimately state Supreme Courts, become the last line of defense for the most fundamental right of all Americans: the right to vote.
In 2025, with support from more than 100,000 grassroots donors, Defend Our Courts helped defend the pro-democracy majority on Pennsylvania's Supreme Court, protecting a critical electoral firewall in one of the country's most important swing states.
Now we are scaling that model nationwide to defend, flip, and build state Supreme Court majorities before the next election cycle. But we cannot do it without YOU.
[If you believe leaders must be held accountable and elections must remain free and fair, please step up today. name: Ilhan Omar
subject: Real quick
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ILHAN FOR CONGRESS
body:
It’s no secret that the last few months have been extremely challenging
# [](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/35?t=1&akid=2087%2E791427%2ElDxpUl)
Friend, I know it’s a Sunday, and I know you get a lot of emails like this from all kinds of folks in your inbox everyday, so I’ll keep this short.
It’s no secret that the last few months have been extremely challenging for Minnesota and my Minneapolis community. But I am more energized than ever before to fight back and defeat the authoritarians in this corrupt administration.
Trump’s attacks, JD Vance’s lies, Kristi Noem’s delusions, ICE’s brutality, the GOP’s sham investigations — none of this will stop us as long as we get organized and stand together in solidarity.
That’s why I’m hoping you will chip in just a few bucks to my campaign today. We’re just past the halfway point of the month, and our fundraising is well behind where we hoped it would be by now. And a slowdown in fundraising leads to a slowdown in our work.
So please: [**Chip in whatever you can afford today and help us get back on track. Let’s build a movement so powerful that it can defeat the far-right, put an end to their extreme agenda, and put our nation on the path towards a future where everyone can thrive.**](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/7?c0=io-em-feb26.5-Kap5&refcode=em260215-1-180&t=2&akid=2087%2E791427%2ElDxpUl)
**[CHIP IN HERE](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/7?c0=io-em-feb26.5-Kap5&refcode=em260215-1-180&t=3&akid=2087%2E791427%2ElDxpUl)**
In solidarity,
— Ilhan

PAID FOR BY ILHAN FOR CONGRESS
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a very important way we communicate with supporters like you, but if you want to, you can **[unsubscribe here](https://act.ilhanomar.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?source=em-footer&t=4&akid=2087%2E791427%2ElDxpUl)**. You can also **[sign up to receive fewer emails](https://act.ilhanomar.com/signup/fewer-emails/?source=em-footer&t=5&akid=2087%2E791427%2ElDxpUl)**.
Submit this form to [**update your contact information**](https://act.ilhanomar.com/signup/update-contact-info/source=em-footer?t=6&akid=2087%2E791427%2ElDxpUl)
Ilhan is counting on our people-powered grassroots movement to fund this campaign. If you can, stand with us by **[making a contribution today](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/io-em-footer/?refcode=em-footer&t=7&refcode2=2087_791427_lDxpUl&akid=2087%2E791427%2ElDxpUl)**.
Ilhan for Congress
PO Box 33079
Washington D.C., 20033

name: Roy Cooper
subject: I can see what’s in store. We can do better.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Heartland Patriots (LucasKunce
body:
I’m asking because it’s important.
Hi Peter —
I hate to have to ask for your help again, but my finance team has let me know we are still not quite where we need to be to hit our February online fundraising target.
Are you able to step up with a contribution split between our campaign and Heartland Patriots to help close the gap? 👇
**[https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hp-cooper-email-20260215?refcode=dpwillis67@gmail.com](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/100589?refcode=em20260215-10056&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=10056%2E614464%2EpOXo72)**
The campaign we’re undertaking here in North Carolina is not one that I can win alone, and it is not one we can afford to lose.
I can see what the GOP has in store for our country Peter, and I think we can do better.
Instead of fomenting crises and stoking division, our leaders can take real action to bring opportunity to folks here at home.
That’s why, when I was Governor, we focused on creating jobs, bringing down health care costs, and investing in our communities.
I’m running for the U.S. Senate to do the same for Americans everywhere. But I’m relying on your help to make our campaign a success. Please, give before midnight tonight to help keep our fundraising on track for the month.
Here’s the link again: **[https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hp-cooper-email-20260215?refcode=dpwillis67@gmail.com](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/100589?refcode=em20260215-10056&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=10056%2E614464%2EpOXo72)**
I appreciate it,
Roy Cooper

| [](https://www.facebook.com/lucaskuncemo/) | [](https://twitter.com/lucaskuncemo) | [](https://www.instagram.com/lucaskuncemo/)
---|---|---

name: Demand Justice PAC
subject: Before Trump’s revenge tour escalates:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Demand Justice PAC
body:
[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/54940?refcode=em20260215-61172&t=1001&akid=61172%2E1407376%2ExHr3su ]Demand Justice PAC[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/54940?refcode=em20260215-61172&t=1002&akid=61172%2E1407376%2ExHr3su ]Demand Justice PAC

Trump promised revenge — and he’s delivering it with a vengeance.

Since returning to the White House, Trump has turned the Department of
Justice into a personal instrument of retaliation, targeting anyone who
dares stand in his way.

New York Attorney General Letitia James? Targeted not for breaking the
law, but for holding Trump accountable for civil fraud.

Former FBI Director James Comey? Targeted for refusing to bow to Trump’s
lies and corruption.

Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell? Targeted simply for doing his job and
following the facts, rather than bending them to Trump’s political will.

It’s authoritarianism in real time. He’s weaponizing the government and
the justice system to intimidate, punish, and silence anyone who refuses
to play by his rules. And if we do nothing, no one is safe.

Demand Justice PAC refuses to stay silent. We are sounding the alarm and
calling out this abuse of power before it becomes normalized. We’re
calling on everyone who believes in justice, democracy, and accountability
to act — before Trump’s revenge tour escalates further.

[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/55459?c0=dj-em-20260215.EmnjC4&refcode=em20260215-61172&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1003&akid=61172%2E1407376%2ExHr3su ]Stand with us. Chip in $5 to Demand Justice PAC today
and say no to a justice system twisted into a tool of revenge.

In solidarity,

Demand Justice PAC

name: Deb Haaland
subject: is there anything I can say?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY DEB FOR NEW MEXICO**
body:
| | | [](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/1102?refcode=em-header%3Frefcode%3Djtk267-fr-nd&t=1&akid=7446%2E400544%2E3qS1hb)
---
Hi, friend, it's Deb with an urgent question:

** _[Is there ANYTHING I can say to convince you to donate ANY amount — before my mid-month fundraising deadline — to help me win the New Mexico governor's race?](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/4677?refcode=jtk267-fr-nd&t=2&akid=7446%2E400544%2E3qS1hb)_**
[](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/4677?refcode=jtk267-fr-nd&t=3&akid=7446%2E400544%2E3qS1hb)
| | [Donate](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/4677?refcode=jtk267-fr-nd&t=4&akid=7446%2E400544%2E3qS1hb)
---
What if I told you that election year is officially here, and our campaign is operating with fewer resources than we need to compete in one of the largest states in the country?
What if I told you that MAGA forces with deep pockets will spend whatever it takes to BUY this seat and flip our state red?
What if I told you that there are now five Republicans running to flip New Mexico and several of them are using their own wealth to try to buy this race and bring an extreme agenda into our state, making it clear that we'll need a surge of grassroots support to stay ahead?
What if I told you that now more than ever, Democratic governors are essential to serve as a backstop when the federal government abuses its resources and undermines civil liberties?
**And what if I told you that national Republicans are already eyeing this race and watching to see if we have the grassroots power to win?**
**Friend, I know we've got what it takes to win — but I can only get there if committed grassroots supporters like you help me reach every single goal from now through Election Day, including this one.**
**With time running out, I have to ask:_[Will you step up with a donation of $5, or anything at all, to help me reach my $30,000 mid-month goal, win in New Mexico, and keep our state blue?](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/4677?refcode=jtk267-fr-nd&t=5&akid=7446%2E400544%2E3qS1hb)_**

name: H. Stevens
subject: Now's our chance, null
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Haley Stevens for Senate
body:
I have to ask:

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. [To unsubscribe from this email list, please click here.](https://act.haleyformi.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?&t=1&akid=2247%2E49239%2EpuShpw)
| | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/14055?t=2&akid=2247%2E49239%2EpuShpw)
---
Null, this is a longer email than I usually send. **But before I ask you to[ _chip in $5_](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/14056?t=3&akid=2247%2E49239%2EpuShpw), I'd like to tell you about my race in Michigan, why I'm running, and why I'm asking for your support.**
I'm running because I'm a lifelong Michigander who has spent my career fighting for our families, our small businesses, and our manufacturing economy. Now, I'm ready to bring this fight to the Senate at this critical moment for Michigan and the country.
**Flipping the Senate blue is Democrats' best chance of keeping the Trump administration in check – and we simply can't do that if we lose here in Michigan.
**
MAGA Republicans know this too, and that's why they're laser-focused on this race. After spending nearly $80 million here last cycle, they're gearing up to spend even more in 2026, **putting this on track to be one of the most competitive – and expensive – races in the country.**
**[_That's why I urgently need your help, null._](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/14057?t=4&akid=2247%2E49239%2EpuShpw) My crucial mid-month deadline is tonight, and right now, we're pacing behind where we need to be.**
This is a really important moment for our grassroots campaign. This is our chance to show the strength of our movement to my opponent and his megadonors. **[_I hope I can count on your support._](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/14057?t=5&akid=2247%2E49239%2EpuShpw)**
**The bottom line is I can't run the most competitive campaign, win this seat, and take our fight for working families to the Senate if I don't have the resources to keep up with GOP spending and fight back against their attacks.
**
**[_So, null, I have to ask: Will you rush $5 or more to help us reach our $175,000 goal before 11:59 p.m. tonight?_](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/14057?t=6&akid=2247%2E49239%2EpuShpw)**
_Please use the links in this email to start a monthly donation through ActBlue:_ | | | [Donate $5](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/14056?t=7&akid=2247%2E49239%2EpuShpw)
---
| | [Donate $25](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/14058?t=8&akid=2247%2E49239%2EpuShpw)
---
| | [Donate $50](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/14059?t=9&akid=2247%2E49239%2EpuShpw)
---
| | [Donate Another Amount](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/14057?t=10&akid=2247%2E49239%2EpuShpw)
---
Thanks for reading to the end of this longer email, and thank you in advance for giving what you can. It means everything to have you with me in this fight.

Haley Stevens
|
* * *
**A note from Haley Stevens:** null, I’m proud to be a lifelong Michigander and the daughter of small-business owners. That’s why I’ve fought so hard for working families my entire life. Now, I’m running for Michigan’s open Senate seat to continue fighting for the people and communities I love. The path to the Senate majority runs right through Michigan, which means this race is shaping up to be the most competitive and expensive in the entire country. The GOP is already coming after me as a top target for defeat, so I’m counting on your grassroots support to fight back and win. [**If you’re in this fight with me, will you add your name to endorse my campaign?**](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/14021?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=11&akid=2247%2E49239%2EpuShpw) | | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/14055?t=12&akid=2247%2E49239%2EpuShpw)
---
You can also text “JOIN” to 80242 to become a core member of our team and receive exclusive, real-time updates from my campaign.
Thank you,
Haley
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
[To unsubscribe from this email list, please click here.](https://act.haleyformi.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?&t=13&akid=2247%2E49239%2EpuShpw)
Paid for by Haley Stevens for Senate.
33717 Woodward Ave #539
Birmingham, MI 48009

subject: Wow, wow, wow
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY SCHIFF FOR SENATE
body:
| This is a key moment, Derek
Wow, wow, wow, Derek.
First, experts at _The Cook Political Report_ increased our chances of winning in Ohio — a state we simply must win to take back the Senate.
Then, a poll showed me ahead of my Republican opponent. Our grassroots movement really started to gain steam.
**Now, ANOTHER POLL shows that I’m leading this crucial race!**
**Here’s the problem:** Corporate special interests are going to spend MILLIONS to defeat me and silence our pro-worker message — and misleading attack ads have already been launched. **We need to fight back NOW.**

name: Lisa Blunt Rochester
subject: Why this Black History Month matters
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Lisa Blunt Rochester for Senate
body:

Team, it’s Lisa.

This Black History Month, I’ve been thinking about what it really means to make history, and just how fragile that progress can be.

When Shirley Chisholm became the first Black woman elected to Congress, she opened doors that had been closed for generations. Her courage changed what was possible — not just for Black women, but for the nation as a whole.

She was the first of 64 Black women who’ve served in Congress. But decades later, **only five Black women have ever served in the United States Senate.**

__Five.__

That number should give us all pause.

As we celebrate this Black History Month, we’re also facing the GOP’s escalated attempts to erase it. Donald Trump and his extremist allies are pushing policies that silence voices and roll back hard-won progress.

That’s exactly why I want to ask: [**_Can you chip in $10 or more today to help elect more Black women leaders and protect the progress we’ve made?_**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/125444976/601811863/-489009331?amount=10&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2026.02.15_Black-History-Month_B1_F1_S1_C1_U1_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85Mjg3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2YzMxMzgwZC04YTBhLWYxMTEtODMyZS0wMDIyNDgyYTk3MzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=0UYFW3H9XgoLFDL8p0CNFjD_CONU9xscn5lGeIyafj4=) | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:

name: Ilhan Omar for Congress
subject: $5 for Ilhan
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Justice Democrats
body:
#
Peter, we’re reaching out to humbly ask you to split a $5 contribution between Ilhan Omar’s campaign for reelection and Justice Democrats.
Yes, just $5.
$5 because Minneapolis and all of Minnesota have been engaged in a historic struggle for civil rights and basic human decency while ICE wreaked havoc our state.
$5 because Ilhan has been a consistent leader on opposing ICE’s brutality, defunding this corrupt agency, holding ICE agents criminally accountable for wrongdoing, and impeaching DHS Secretary Kristi Noem.
$5 because despite everything, Ilhan still has a campaign to run and a race to win, and we can’t afford to let our fundraising slip and send a signal to AIPAC that they can swoop in and buy this race.
Peter, Ilhan relies on grassroots donations from folks like you. Not billionaires, Super PACs, or corporate lobbyists.
**[So please: Split a contribution of $5 or any other amount today and stand with Ilhan Omar in this important moment for Minnesota. We’ve always been able to count on this grassroots team to show up when we need it most. Chip in here:](https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20260215-io.aao93w&refcode=em20260215-37464&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=2&akid=37464%2E1203867%2EgXHsTq)**

In solidarity,
Team Ilhan

Friend,
The war on President Trump started the instant he stepped off that golden escalator in 2015 and promised to put America First.
**The Democrat Machine, their media pawns, and entrenched Deep State bureaucrats declared total war with no rules and no mercy.**
They hit him with the **Russia hoax** that dragged on for years, costing hundreds of millions and tearing the country apart.
When that collapsed, they pivoted straight to the **impeachment fraud** – a manufactured crisis built on lies, designed to nullify your vote and remove a duly elected President.
And even after they lost, they didn’t stop. They kept coming – _**more investigations, lawfare traps, endless smears, corrupt indictments and convictions.**_
Through all this and worse, President Trump survived, persevered, and prevailed – winning a stunning comeback for a second term to help save this country we love so dearly.
**_But the radicals never quit. They’re still burning with rage._**
** And now, Alexander Vindman – the man who helped to orchestrate the entire failed impeachment hoax and nearly killed MAGA – is back. And he's out for cold, hard revenge. **
**He just launched a campaign to hijack Florida's critical U.S. Senate seat in one of this year’s biggest special elections. And within 24 hours, liberal donors pumped in an eye-popping $1.7 million with much more on the way.**
**This is no longer a normal race._It is straight, personal revenge politics._**

---

**This morning, my wife Anna and I had the incredible privilege of soaking in Downtown Mobile --the true birthplace of Mardi Gras--and all its majestic charm. As someone born and raised here, Mobile holds a special place in my heart. It's where I grew up, where I met Anna, where we've raised our children, and where I've built a career serving customers and the community. There's an undeniable magic here that draws you in, from the historic streets to the warm spirit that seeps from every giant live oak.**
**My pride in Mobile runs deep. This is a truly unique and diverse area, home to some of the hardest-working, most innovative, and outside-the-box thinkers you 'll ever encounter. The people here embody resilience and creativity, turning challenges into opportunities time and again. But that pride doesn't mean turning a blind eye to our history. I'm committed to looking back with clear eyes, always open to learning more and striving for improvement--not just for Mobile, but for all of Alabama.**
**Our state has a painful past, one steeped in the horrors of slavery and segregation. Those shadows haven 't fully faded; they've lingered into the present, shaping perceptions and policies in ways that continue to hold us back. Too often, the rest of the nation has dismissed Alabama as "not worth saving," using our history as an excuse to overlook our potential. Yet, that very past has circled back, influencing the challenges we face today. We're being defined not by our people's true values, but by corrupt politicians who fail to represent a brighter future.**
**These leaders thrive on division, wielding fear as a weapon to pit us against one another. They distort reality by villainizing immigrants --particularly black and brown communities--as scapegoats for our problems. This tactic isn't just misleading; it's a deliberate distraction. While they stoke these flames, corruption festers unchecked, and their economic policies squeeze the life out of hardworking families across the state. The people of Alabama are struggling under the weight of decisions that prioritize power over progress.**
**For many of us, including myself, ignoring the scars of history isn 't an option. Our past shouldn't define our future. Instead, it can serve as a profound teacher, guiding us toward becoming the best versions of ourselves. It requires WORK and a commitment to Life, Liberty, and the Pursuit of Happiness.**
**Together, we can build a better future, turning lessons from yesterday into a brighter tomorrow for every Alabamian.**
**Hope will always triumph over fear.**
[ Join Us!](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/4a3a8656-4abc-4dba-b129-1cb85b31a155?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

name: Team WFP
subject: Recent victories, and the fights ahead
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Working Families Party PAC (77 Sands St
body:
It’s time for this month's WFP newsletter: | | | Peter, It’s time for the February WFP newsletter, your opportunity to catch up on the work we’re doing around the country to make our government work for working people, as well as protect our communities against attacks by the Trump Administration. Let’s dive in:
* * *
**_Newly Elected WFP Champions Take Office_** Over the last six weeks, Working Families Party champions across the country have been inaugurated to office following their victories in November. [](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoZm3YaiVTCYR0nbWPfhh-NYQQvrRxjWJz040x1wIz61o-gq4-0RWww_SFxFgI7LYKIQrJMJHbTWr9Tj1qYOynN-etZqCnd6-hntaOpCJBkCOBvy-fBlFjJWVPHzS1rAMMF52Qn9kCv2YVDW8G2KjwPHpRc6ZoxRfqe0pWYh-kwbx3kT4tipots5g9sgAHnOYBRUE0F707RWtfndOnPLuhife0wCtvGeil0i2K3_fv4nYJNXZdrgRwxKB8H--oqw_WCnSXCPTAPojTIBQhNYFaMalLyesKlPZQOtLA0pPufZrGfZ0apMiUnUhOPOoJAHafTksVerfBz8r4upETCX1A47hY5wcD-8ew6GVDuDoRaYcehAA8RTrIPVe9AFshPRIGflIBP8KP4zG6cm7HXYLyRY/4o6/lK1TcuMbTLSQUVBXj2sejA/h0/h001.0QTLgJ6Ob0XeO0fIXxLcwmPL9Vu7S6f1GNxDhBtYzXo) Since being inaugurated:
* NYC’s **Zohran Mamdani** has revived the Mayor’s Office to Protect Tenants, announced a $2.1 million settlement that holds bad landlords accountable, banned “junk fees” on hotel stays, and a whole lot more.
* Seattle Mayor **Katie Wilson** has come out strongly against ICE, announcing a ban on agents using city property to conduct operations and ordering the police department to investigate their activities. She's also announced executive orders that would help homeless Seattleites get shelter and housing, as well as improve the city’s public transit.
* And Jersey City Mayor **James Solomon** announced health insurance savings for 5,000 city employees and retirees, and signed an executive order to strengthen the city’s protections for immigrants — also including banning city property from being used for raids.

This is just some of what we’ve seen from our newly elected WFP champions since taking office — and they're just getting started. We're so proud of every member of our movement who is doing the hard work of building a government that works for the many, not the few.
* * *
**_Fighting Back Against ICE Abuses_** In Minnesota and all across the country, working people have been courageously resisting ICE’s violence and cruelty in their communities. Thousands of you have joined protests, become trained as observers, and called and emailed your Senators successfully urging them to vote against the recent DHS funding bill. [](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMYd6eLP33O2GRZpvuK-wGuRi5RSBzueG5nfDSYhydy-othB8pD9BfKuj2DJe0bLMaihjdMqYPPm5UPZLlziC8zCScrr6uDmoHAFgITFYEak2rKDUxuCQhevsSjP5oTbisvV_2c53EsAmTopL-aq5iKMDNhaSF6x3geptoCCZqSVn-sLVNvIfJ3CCO3N9UeBRGSKBXNBP9MADMyBA-Z-te3RD0vpiohdjLB2aLmRIkVlQmEHy3ctBfiB9KM9sRLB08XyskszWfWutlxFmc3defd4/4o6/lK1TcuMbTLSQUVBXj2sejA/h1/h001.L07vB8-M79OCrAhEVemVR4-Q-ekX4hVz61jANv3-VP8) This moment is pivotal; our job is to keep up the pressure. As we continue to fight for a government that will abolish ICE and end their terror for good, we need to work together and take immediate action to stop further harm and support Minnesotans and others who are under attack right now. We demand that ICE and CBP get out of ALL of our communities, no more warrantless arrests and home invasions, an end to funding family detention, and accountability for ICE’s criminal attacks, including the murders of Renee Good and Alex Pretti. Visit **[wfpus.org/iceout](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMYd6eLP33O2GRZpvuK-wGuRi5RSBzueG5nfDSYhydy-othB8pD9BfKuj2DJe0bLMaihjdMqYPPm5UPZLlziC8zAAUP-oKAcbnA7qa3FuCvz2K_Fkrpk2lNiK48HDctd_G0-Cuuhqbuyb5TCJrAIrLIagLgS9OLUrAod-SV-FQ48ndkS1PaUHKzGV1z1rQFR3PzEyqaTyIj4omao7o8nJPuQPQZCm6WZhQc1Ex4Wtk9VleO49J1EnqdgBpBO9YNhQi_DujQFg0FmOF-aQD3AS3iY/4o6/lK1TcuMbTLSQUVBXj2sejA/h2/h001.ab7xpfBOAb-gUaLoSLM_dFKVooQVf2JSe-pilzWgdwQ)** for ways to take action and support communities under attack as they stand up to ICE's abuses.

**_Elections Update: Big WFP Victories To Kick Off A Big 2026_** [](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMam-uDCpDA0_SaNpoOF9ZDEMTDfa97kUouGZu69x18rAiwRqAVpHMV_h3wbgkXqodxqfE0R3gE0eWveDSuYL8bvTIrnmJgwbWFLdh7aSUgBHQQ-Z2iHgPsTR471cMZm-GWjsvMhC3ZeS0SelJKDuUYO_3BgDxAdAN1aYz0NXWBbQ9zJOoCMn_5HHTbfGVTKNjSBRg-M8QfcbXN1zTlzmExtr9_ejcovndPaeLGxnc69Twc1DLiJpLAEYOlxqMb-qhxYjzRYqxIR4ir3xKD_TNh7SSvNLyZxOK20cJrQIAw5nFC_XU9EIBI3bNBN0z_k69P69BkEzAv5F5pYgKfC8-WUexuTnlUvzEvg2BH8wgg4ymr4B9cY1R-NYyYltNcJ8fw/4o6/lK1TcuMbTLSQUVBXj2sejA/h3/h001.nwEfwUmyD0UePWTRVbypmfXqeGvPcTNlGQmTrfNujMM) 2026 is less than two months old, but we've already kicked off the year with two big election wins: This week, former **New Jersey Working Families Party** State Director **Analilia Mejia** was declared the winner in her special primary election for New Jersey’s 11th Congressional District. Her victory is one more piece of evidence that working people are sick and tired of the status quo and hungry for real leadership that stands up for workers’ rights, economic justice, and a democracy that belongs to everyday people, not corporate interests. The **Texas Working Families Party** also endorsed and worked to get out the vote for **Taylor Rehmet,** a machinist and union leader who won his State Senate runoff in late January in a district that went for Trump by 17 points in 2024. This election showed that when we fight for affordability and don’t give up, we can win over voters in deep red districts and build a government that works for all of us. Looking ahead: It's officially midterm season, Peter! And we're starting to assemble a slate of bold congressional candidates who are ready to take on the challenges of the second Trump administration, including:
* **Brad Lander,** the outgoing New York City Comptroller and longtime **New York Working Families Party** champion, who is running for Congress in New York’s 10th Congressional District. Brad has stood up for our communities for years, including putting his body on the line in the face of ICE’s abuses last year.
* **Nida Allam,** a leading advocate for affordable housing, guaranteed healthcare, and a fair living wage, who is running in North Carolina’s 4th Congressional District. We’re going all in for Nida as she faces a rematch against incumbent crypto and AIPAC-backed Rep. Valerie Foushee in a primary coming up soon on March 3rd.
* **Robert White,** the **DC Working Families Party** candidate for Delegate to Congress from Washington, D.C., who would work tirelessly to advocate for D.C. statehood, federal investments in working families, and defending the District against political interference and gamesmanship.

We’ve also rolled out our first endorsements for candidates running for state and local elections, including:
* **Jane Kim,** the recent **California Working Families Party** State Director, who is running for State Insurance Commissioner to ensure that everyone can receive affordable coverage,
* **Janeese Lewis George,** WFP candidate for Mayor of D.C., who is running to focus on keeping families in their homes, connect people to good-paying jobs, support small businesses, and protect D.C.’s right to govern itself
* **Aparna Raj, Zachary Parker, Charles Allen, and Oye Olowela,** our WFP slate for D.C. District Council. With unprecedented turnover on the D.C. Council, this election represents a once-in-a-generation opportunity to build governing power for working people in the District.

This is just the start of our 2026 endorsements, and there's a whole lot more to come. **[Find an event here and help get out the vote for our slate of WFP champions running across the country](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMbGj-ca0TYSsYMZBvIweYdJdMRKpuhAT1OnS47U4pbhVX2jLfR2-d7ssnVaByRbX52m5GM834krIj22Gvpv6_-_wEPp3XQHLXLql3Vux1vtLueunqMplSlURh0B74SQxPCnQxq1I4RYmqevfHHXz9fQkS8Ac5D1rXVjRyIykNzDNuOcHZgpWjxv7jWyrMD48ei6Fd6QaJ4nVmtYh5auMjtsjb40GvJQUtytJiMDrwUz93fFyyLR1aiQWCR-06mnu6IpZlSDCWyUf8aE8vwfrTWc/4o6/lK1TcuMbTLSQUVBXj2sejA/h4/h001.Pwm7Vvzk63advyq6enRqN3qssh8-cDeIJslR9droZHU)** — and stay tuned!

**_WFP Hosts “Bad Bunny Bowl” Watch Parties_** When the NFL announced that Bad Bunny would headline the Halftime Show at Super Bowl LX on February 8th, the right wing immediately lost it. Why? Because Benito Antonio Martínez Ocasio (Bad Bunny) has been outspoken against Trump-era policies and even chose not to bring his “No Me Quiero Ir de Aquí” tour to the United States out of concern for the Trump administration’s aggressive deportation agenda. Some critics even called on Marco Rubio to block his “visa” (spoiler alert: Puerto Ricans are U.S. citizens because of their colonial status). **_WFP Champions Fight to Tax the Rich Across The Country_** In January, over 20 **Rhode Island Working Families Party** lawmakers unveiled their Fair Share for Rhode Island Package, a set of four bills to ensure the wealthiest people in the state pay a comparable share of their income in taxes as the rest of Rhode Island filers. Estimated to raise $3 billion over the next five years, this revenue is desperately needed to adequately fund public schools, hospitals, childcare centers, roads, bridges, and public transit. The package is the boldest the state has seen to date with lawmakers making clear that Fair Share is their top legislative priority for the 2026 session. [](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMWn3R7U15hZOprFI75oZg44LT-deIP6HOl0DL8TQLkaAemM4iUpCjFyIbdpYSDWzw920BVwl5-gQLq8Uu8Tr87MFWlsJwl6BcqIjqfD6nnhwvt6_a5IxxY9xycp2qFh27BvFbFYu49AxYB9U431suMUCJnITNsQAUI7koofQLzxBlaj6nQugW7xdpoyzO-e2mLvShUMD2dyknLFXLuBr9FS5OJH28XZcwaGLDljtbc29u_q1PNn9hs-hFmo_cwGhXXCP0Fw5AFF4XQkLB-tKZEw/4o6/lK1TcuMbTLSQUVBXj2sejA/h9/h001.HMwrf8luKqq6D6p1u6U9q3SjMACmHVZuOacpMkSSIrY) [](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMWn3R7U15hZOprFI75oZg44LT-deIP6HOl0DL8TQLkaA7rc0hCWbx8febRCe426FH7_lLQ9OeYU9UKlEblcu8RPntJfaOYuKt6oIvqWJ7pZwfzZUey3MDN0ArAOxVTyuiNtyQ4C1RhDzv-6xs8Upqpw5qtmBoFs3aM9hKAS8i6jQdG5xQPgaEaVYX-NF_dA734o2q9aB0RSawnpiP3fcaqcsaOnp4GrbTtuJfEvUlgVmLVFxFT28Ammx5PNhHLgxyNLBqUUtPGcKsAQlEYIuG-E/4o6/lK1TcuMbTLSQUVBXj2sejA/h10/h001.sFhs2SDqlGKPNNnDBpkaKZlBfiDtRhhOOZ5MurXU8II) WFP activists and elected officials are keeping up the fight to tax the rich across the country:
* The **Oregon Working Families Party** is putting revenue at the top of the agenda for that state’s upcoming legislative session.
* The **Pennsylvania Working Families Party** is a key partner in the “Tax Billionaires, Fund PA” campaign.
* The **Connecticut Working Families Party** continues to advocate for higher revenue in one of the country's richest states.
* And the **Colorado Working Families Party** is fighting back against TABOR, a long-standing state law used by the wealthy and corporations to avoid paying their fair share in taxes.

We know that in order to make our communities affordable, we have to make the rich pay their fair share instead of hoarding wealth and dodging taxes. We’re so grateful to WFP members everywhere who are fighting to make our government work for working people.

clean_body: It’s time for this month's WFP newsletter: | | | Peter, It’s time for the February WFP newsletter, your opportunity to catch up on the work we’re doing around the country to make our government work for working people, as well as protect our communities against attacks by the Trump Administration. Let’s dive in: Newly Elected WFP Champions Take Office Over the last six weeks, Working Families Party champions across the country have been inaugurated to office following their victories in November. Since being inaugurated: NYC’s Zohran Mamdani has revived the Mayor’s Office to Protect Tenants, announced a $2.1 million settlement that holds bad landlords accountable, banned “junk fees” on hotel stays, and a whole lot more. Seattle Mayor Katie Wilson has come out strongly against ICE, announcing a ban on agents using city property to conduct operations and ordering the police department to investigate their activities. She's also announced executive orders that would help homeless Seattleites get shelter and housing, as well as improve the city’s public transit. And Jersey City Mayor James Solomon announced health insurance savings for 5,000 city employees and retirees, and signed an executive order to strengthen the city’s protections for immigrants — also including banning city property from being used for raids. This is just some of what we’ve seen from our newly elected WFP champions since taking office — and they're just getting started. We're so proud of every member of our movement who is doing the hard work of building a government that works for the many, not the few. Fighting Back Against ICE Abuses In Minnesota and all across the country, working people have been courageously resisting ICE’s violence and cruelty in their communities. Thousands of you have joined protests, become trained as observers, and called and emailed your Senators successfully urging them to vote against the recent DHS funding bill. This moment is pivotal; our job is to keep up the pressure. As we continue to fight for a government that will abolish ICE and end their terror for good, we need to work together and take immediate action to stop further harm and support Minnesotans and others who are under attack right now. We demand that ICE and CBP get out of ALL of our communities, no more warrantless arrests and home invasions, an end to funding family detention, and accountability for ICE’s criminal attacks, including the murders of Renee Good and Alex Pretti. Visit for ways to take action and support communities under attack as they stand up to ICE's abuses. Elections Update: Big WFP Victories To Kick Off A Big 2026 2026 is less than two months old, but we've already kicked off the year with two big election wins: This week, former New Jersey Working Families Party State Director Analilia Mejia was declared the winner in her special primary election for New Jersey’s 11th Congressional District. Her victory is one more piece of evidence that working people are sick and tired of the status quo and hungry for real leadership that stands up for workers’ rights, economic justice, and a democracy that belongs to everyday people, not corporate interests. The Texas Working Families Party also endorsed and worked to get out the vote for Taylor Rehmet, a machinist and union leader who won his State Senate runoff in late January in a district that went for Trump by 17 points in 2024. This election showed that when we fight for affordability and don’t give up, we can win over voters in deep red districts and build a government that works for all of us. Looking ahead: It's officially midterm season, Peter! And we're starting to assemble a slate of bold congressional candidates who are ready to take on the challenges of the second Trump administration, including: Brad Lander, the outgoing New York City Comptroller and longtime New York Working Families Party champion, who is running for Congress in New York’s 10th Congressional District. Brad has stood up for our communities for years, including putting his body on the line in the face of ICE’s abuses last year. Nida Allam, a leading advocate for affordable housing, guaranteed healthcare, and a fair living wage, who is running in North Carolina’s 4th Congressional District. We’re going all in for Nida as she faces a rematch against incumbent crypto and AIPAC-backed Rep. Valerie Foushee in a primary coming up soon on March 3rd. Robert White, the DC Working Families Party candidate for Delegate to Congress from Washington, D.C., who would work tirelessly to advocate for D.C. statehood, federal investments in working families, and defending the District against political interference and gamesmanship. We’ve also rolled out our first endorsements for candidates running for state and local elections, including: Jane Kim, the recent California Working Families Party State Director, who is running for State Insurance Commissioner to ensure that everyone can receive affordable coverage, Janeese Lewis George, WFP candidate for Mayor of D.C., who is running to focus on keeping families in their homes, connect people to good-paying jobs, support small businesses, and protect D.C.’s right to govern itself Aparna Raj, Zachary Parker, Charles Allen, and Oye Olowela, our WFP slate for D.C. District Council. With unprecedented turnover on the D.C. Council, this election represents a once-in-a-generation opportunity to build governing power for working people in the District. This is just the start of our 2026 endorsements, and there's a whole lot more to come. — and stay tuned! WFP Hosts “Bad Bunny Bowl” Watch Parties When the NFL announced that Bad Bunny would headline the Halftime Show at Super Bowl LX on February 8th, the right wing immediately lost it. Why? Because Benito Antonio Martínez Ocasio (Bad Bunny) has been outspoken against Trump-era policies and even chose not to bring his “No Me Quiero Ir de Aquí” tour to the United States out of concern for the Trump administration’s aggressive deportation agenda. Some critics even called on Marco Rubio to block his “visa” (spoiler alert: Puerto Ricans are U.S. citizens because of their colonial status). But hundreds ignored the bigots and celebrated Bad Bunny’s performance as a celebration of Puerto Rican pride, Latinidad, and resistance at WFP-hosted watch parties in California, Pennsylvania, Georgia, Ohio, and New Jersey! Read more at . ICE OUT At the Grammys Meanwhile, Working Families Power joined forces with artists at the Grammy Awards to send a powerful message that ICE does not belong in our communities. Artists including Billie Eilish, Bon Iver, Jack Antonoff, Margo Price, Samara Joy, Kehlani, Justin and Hailey Bieber, and more wore “ICE OUT” pins, using their platform at an event watched by millions to show their opposition to ICE’s violent attacks and terror. Artists also took a stand during their speeches at the Grammys, with Bad Bunny demanding “ICE out” in his acceptance speech for best música urbána album. Working Families Power’s Nelini Stamp told reporters: “We need every part of civil society to speak up. We need our artists. We need our entertainers. We need the folks who reflect society.” WFP Champions Fight to Tax the Rich Across The Country In January, over 20 Rhode Island Working Families Party lawmakers unveiled their Fair Share for Rhode Island Package, a set of four bills to ensure the wealthiest people in the state pay a comparable share of their income in taxes as the rest of Rhode Island filers. Estimated to raise $3 billion over the next five years, this revenue is desperately needed to adequately fund public schools, hospitals, childcare centers, roads, bridges, and public transit. The package is the boldest the state has seen to date with lawmakers making clear that Fair Share is their top legislative priority for the 2026 session. WFP activists and elected officials are keeping up the fight to tax the rich across the country: The Oregon Working Families Party is putting revenue at the top of the agenda for that state’s upcoming legislative session. The Pennsylvania Working Families Party is a key partner in the “Tax Billionaires, Fund PA” campaign. The Connecticut Working Families Party continues to advocate for higher revenue in one of the country's richest states. And the Colorado Working Families Party is fighting back against TABOR, a long-standing state law used by the wealthy and corporations to avoid paying their fair share in taxes. We know that in order to make our communities affordable, we have to make the rich pay their fair share instead of hoarding wealth and dodging taxes. We’re so grateful to WFP members everywhere who are fighting to make our government work for working people. From The Guardian: “Working Families bet on 2026 as the right time for a third US party after a wave of wins” An article in The Guardian recently laid out our ambitious goals for 2026 and our long-term work to build a powerful third party by and for the multiracial working class: Here’s an excerpt: Could the US have a true third party? [WFP National Director Maurice] Mitchell said he wouldn’t do the work if he didn’t think it was “both necessary and possible”. The party is also working to dismantle structural barriers that make the rise of third parties difficult in the US, he said. “Less and less people are identifying as being a Democrat or Republican,” he said. “The brand of the Democratic and the Republican parties are underwater consistently. I don’t think there’s been a better and more right time for a third party to emerge in this country that speaks to the interest of everyday working people. I believe that our time has come.” Upcoming Events Looking for a way to get involved with WFP and have an hour to spare? Join our weekly WFP Call Party Power Hour to make calls with your fellow WFP volunteers from anywhere! Join the upcoming Power Hour next Wednesday, February 18th at 7PM ET/4 PM PT. Whether you’re a long-time WFP member or just getting started, we’re inviting you to join our February Welcome Gathering! At the Welcome Gathering, WFP members and activists will get concrete about how we’re building the power we need to have an impact in this political moment, and help you figure out the best ways to get involved. Right now, Congress is negotiating on whether the government will give even more tax dollars to the Department of Homeland Security for their campaign of terror in our communities. Join us to learn how we can use our voices to tell Congress to get ICE out now. Thank you for reading the WFP February Newsletter! If you’re feeling inspired by the work members and activists like you are doing across the country, we ask you to Thank you for being a part of our movement. In solidarity, Team WFP Thoughts? Feedback? We want to hear it. Reply directly to this email and let us know. This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you received this email as a forward and want to get the latest updates from the Working Families Party directly, The Working Families Party is the party of the multiracial working class, fighting for a nation that cares for all of us. Wanna learn more? And if you need to update your contact information, Other political groups have corporate donors. But we can pool our resources too. Paid for by Working Families Party PAC (77 Sands St. #6, Brooklyn, NY, 11201). | | Sent via . To update your email address, change your name or address, or to stop receiving emails from Working Families Party and Working Families Power, please .

name: Gina Hinojosa
subject: It’s time to take back Texas
body:
Are you with me, Peter?
# [](https://ginafortexas.com/?refcode=em_ms_gh_header)[](https://ginafortexas.com/?refcode=em_ms_gh_header)
Peter, this campaign is taking us back to our populist roots in Texas.
We have a broken system in Texas that is being used against the vast majority of us, whether we’re Democrats or Republicans. And that system has been specially designed to serve no one but the top 1% of Greg Abbott’s wealthiest donors.
That means that Texans are all in the same boat. And we need a populist agenda to fight back against the corrupt system that is stealing our future from us.
**Populism is in our historical roots, Peter.**
LBJ’s grandfather was part of the Farmers’ Alliance that pushed back against the big railroads because the people of Texas were being taken advantage of.
The same thing is happening today — wealthy donors and special interests are fleecing us for their own gain.
So in this campaign, we’re reaching back to our past and doing something quintessentially Texan: **We’re pursuing an agenda that actually benefits Texans.**
**[Are you with me, Peter? Pitch in $5 now to power this campaign.](https://act.ginafortexas.com/go/625?c0=em_ms_gh_20260215_1_populistroots.HJrtB0&refcode=em20260215-584&amount=5&t=1&akid=584%2E299639%2EaVycp-)**

Thank you for joining the fight.
— Gina

name: Josh Shapiro
subject: Let me explain
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY SHAPIRO FOR PENNSYLVANIA
body:
This is a test of our determination

I don’t want to sound alarmist, but I need you to know: **National Republicans have their eyes on Pennsylvania this year.** They won Pennsylvania in 2024. Now, they think they can do it again — and they’re willing to spend millions to get it done. [_**Derek, I truly believe — when all is said and done — we will win this race on Election Day. But we can’t become overconfident. We can’t sleepwalk our way through this election, so please, can I count on your support before tonight’s deadline?**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/125445858/601814737/-1395620021?refcode=email_021526_mmfr1&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500,1000&amount=35&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NTY5MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhNWI5NjQ0Ny04ZDBhLWYxMTEtODMyZS0wMDIyNDgyYTk3MzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=CrjRTrVmHdFa6P3A5gxWG8vjvVwkFGxpBTA6xqa3xtY=) | | [Donate →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/125445859/601814738/-1395620021?refcode=email_021526_mmfr1&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500,1000&amount=35&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NTY5MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhNWI5NjQ0Ny04ZDBhLWYxMTEtODMyZS0wMDIyNDgyYTk3MzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=CrjRTrVmHdFa6P3A5gxWG8vjvVwkFGxpBTA6xqa3xtY=)
---
Derek, I remember that night. That feeling we all felt when it became clear Donald Trump would win Pennsylvania — and the Presidency — in 2024.
It was a combination of fear, dread, and uncertainty. A feeling that was justified just minutes into his second term.
Many days, I still feel those same emotions. When the President tries to withhold funding from our Commonwealth for the purpose of political retribution.
**But those actions also make me determined, Derek. Determined to keep the President and his enablers' chaos, cruelty, and corruption far, far away from the good people of Pennsylvania.**
Now, I’m hoping you feel that same determination. That unshakeable feeling that if we roll up our sleeves and work as hard as possible over the next nine months, we can win this election. We can continue building on what we’ve accomplished together.
**We can reject far-right extremism once and for all.**
[_**If you’re with me in this fight, I’m counting on your donation, Derek. Because tonight’s deadline is about so much more than some numbers on a spreadsheet. It’s a test of our determination — our willpower — to fight for Pennsylvania’s future.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/125445860/601814739/-1395620021?refcode=email_021526_mmfr1&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500,1000&amount=35&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NTY5MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhNWI5NjQ0Ny04ZDBhLWYxMTEtODMyZS0wMDIyNDgyYTk3MzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=CrjRTrVmHdFa6P3A5gxWG8vjvVwkFGxpBTA6xqa3xtY=)

clean_body: This is a test of our determination I don’t want to sound alarmist, but I need you to know: National Republicans have their eyes on Pennsylvania this year. They won Pennsylvania in 2024. Now, they think they can do it again — and they’re willing to spend millions to get it done. = | | = Derek, I remember that night. That feeling we all felt when it became clear Donald Trump would win Pennsylvania — and the Presidency — in 2024. It was a combination of fear, dread, and uncertainty. A feeling that was justified just minutes into his second term. Many days, I still feel those same emotions. When the President tries to withhold funding from our Commonwealth for the purpose of political retribution. But those actions also make me determined, Derek. Determined to keep the President and his enablers' chaos, cruelty, and corruption far, far away from the good people of Pennsylvania. Now, I’m hoping you feel that same determination. That unshakeable feeling that if we roll up our sleeves and work as hard as possible over the next nine months, we can win this election. We can continue building on what we’ve accomplished together. We can reject far-right extremism once and for all. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | = | = | | | = | = | | | = | = Thank you for reading, Josh Shapiro | By participating, you consent to receive donation asks, voter contact, and informational messages from Shapiro for Pennsylvania. name: Jane Fonda
subject: Hi, it’s Jane
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jane Fonda Climate PAC, not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee
body:
Why I’m reaching out:

| Hi there, I hope you know I wouldn’t be reaching out on a Sunday if it wasn’t important. Today is the last day for my Climate PAC to hit our $20,000 mid-month fundraising goal. Every dollar we bring in helps elect bold candidates with the fearlessness and backbone to take on Donald Trump and the fossil fuel industry — _and win._ I’ll explain why it matters so much that folks like you, in Cresco, contribute before midnight, but if you already have what you need, here’s a quick link to donate: [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jane-bax-email](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/125446061/601815038/2105508926?refcode=ea.4b0f5b1e-8e0a-f111-832e-0022482a9733&ms=ea.4b0f5b1e-8e0a-f111-832e-0022482a9733&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjRiMGY1YjFlLThlMGEtZjExMS04MzJlLTAwMjI0ODJhOTczMyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=3x1X3u_1abbzxKZcWeS5IDl-a9DDaLOYxyBvD5quSVY=) We’re just _three seats away_ from flipping the House of Representatives in November. It won’t be easy. We’re up against Big Oil money, Trump’s authoritarianism, and his entire MAGA machine — essentially, forces that hope we’ll tune out, burn out, or get lost in their chaos. But I’ve seen the strength of this grassroots movement for myself. Over 60 climate champions elected in 2025.
More than 20 seats flipped to brave, values-driven leaders. And heading into the midterms, we’re counting on you once again — because our candidates can win, _if_ we can just get them the resources to compete. _If_ we can flip the House and send these brave leaders to Congress, we can build the firewall our communities desperately need to stop Trump’s giveaways to big polluters _and_ his authoritarian, inhumane attacks on our neighbors. **But the thing is, no one person has ever beaten authoritarianism alone._We have to build a mass movement that no one can ignore._** That means we need communities all over to plug in — including Cresco. So I’m asking: Can I count on your support before 11:59 p.m. tonight to help us reach our $20,000 goal? Your contribution will directly fund the organizing, outreach, and resources our candidates rely on to meet this moment with determination and people-powered strength. Here’s that link to give again: [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jane-bax-email](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/125446062/601815039/2105508926?refcode=ea.4b0f5b1e-8e0a-f111-832e-0022482a9733&ms=ea.4b0f5b1e-8e0a-f111-832e-0022482a9733&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjRiMGY1YjFlLThlMGEtZjExMS04MzJlLTAwMjI0ODJhOTczMyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=3x1X3u_1abbzxKZcWeS5IDl-a9DDaLOYxyBvD5quSVY=) In solidarity, xx Jane

name: Team In This Together PAC
subject: In the wealthiest nation on earth, people shouldn't be struggling like this.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by In This Together PAC
body:
All talk. No action.

_Gov. Andy Beshear said it best, Peter:_
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/125446191/601815652/1338299492?refcode=em26_260215_health_fndd_gq_act___tittpac_ettx&&&&utm_campaign=em26_260215_health_fndd_gq_act___tittpac_ettx&&&&utm_medium=email&&&&refcode2=aecde4cd-8e0a-f111-832e-0022482a9733&refcode2=aecde4cd-8e0a-f111-832e-0022482a9733&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjEvMS8xMTAzNjMiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYWVjZGU0Y2QtOGUwYS1mMTExLTgzMmUtMDAyMjQ4MmE5NzMzIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=sfNvF1TmcsFEtYv5t5rYWklC1R1SwVDqLJsv6qPMSj8=)
**In the wealthiest nation on earth, families are struggling, and Congress is still doing nothing.**

ACA tax credits have expired, hospitals are closing their doors, and people are driving hours just to see a doctor.

_And that's only the tip of the iceberg..._

**Costs are rising, access to care is shrinking, and it's our rural communities who are being forced to deal with the brunt of the fallout.**

Meanwhile, GOP leaders are still repeating the same talking points: the ACA is terrible, it has to go, we need something better. _But when it comes to real solutions?_**They offer nothing — no plan — just excuses.**

**[Andy knows this isn't how leadership works. Talking points mean nothing when you can't deliver for your constituents. We need leaders in Congress who will step up and do the job they were elected to do — and that's exactly the kind of candidate Andy is supporting. He's helping key House races across the country that will be vital in flipping the House and instilling real leadership back into Congress. If you want to stand with him and help his efforts, chip in today>>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/125446192/601815653/1338299492?refcode=em26_260215_health_fndd_gq_act___tittpac_ettx&&&&utm_campaign=em26_260215_health_fndd_gq_act___tittpac_ettx&&&&utm_medium=email&&&&refcode2=aecde4cd-8e0a-f111-832e-0022482a9733&refcode2=aecde4cd-8e0a-f111-832e-0022482a9733&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjEvMS8xMTAzNjMiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYWVjZGU0Y2QtOGUwYS1mMTExLTgzMmUtMDAyMjQ4MmE5NzMzIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=sfNvF1TmcsFEtYv5t5rYWklC1R1SwVDqLJsv6qPMSj8=)** clean_body: All talk. No action. Gov. Andy Beshear said it best, Peter: = In the wealthiest nation on earth, families are struggling, and Congress is still doing nothing. ACA tax credits have expired, hospitals are closing their doors, and people are driving hours just to see a doctor. And that's only the tip of the iceberg... Costs are rising, access to care is shrinking, and it's our rural communities who are being forced to deal with the brunt of the fallout. Meanwhile, GOP leaders are still repeating the same talking points: the ACA is terrible, it has to go, we need something better. But when it comes to real solutions?They offer nothing — no plan — just excuses. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | = | | = ---|---|---|---|--- = | | = | | = ---|---|---|---|--- Thank you, Team In This Together PAC | Kentucky Gov. name: Beto O'Rourke
subject: Where I’m going this week
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Powered by People, [poweredxpeople
body:
# [](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/43706?t=1&akid=240691%2E444211%2ESNsWfm)
I’m heading back on the road with Powered by People this week to continue the work that will help us win these elections in November.
Before I walk you through where we’ll be this week, [**can you chip in $3 so we have the resources to get back on the road?**](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20260215.kxQB0T&refcode=ema20260215-240691&t=3&akid=240691%2E444211%2ESNsWfm)
On Monday, we’ll be at Midwestern State University in Wichita Falls. This has traditionally been a Red county, in fact it’s where Greg Abbott is from, _but that’s exactly why we’re going._ As JFK said in a speech in Texas in 1962, we do these things not because they’re easy, but because they’re hard. That’s what it’s going to take to win Texas. We don’t write anyone off, or take anyone for granted. There are voters in Wichita Falls we know we can win in November if we build relationships with them now.
Tuesday, we’re going to the University of North Texas in Denton. Another Red county, but it’s on the edge of tipping.
If we organize from now all the way through November we can win it. On Wednesday, we’ll be back in Tarrant County at the University of Texas at Arlington. This is known as the last reliably Red urban county in America, but our work is changing that. **High turnout powered Taylor Rehmet’s stunning victory in Tarrant County just a few weeks ago, and a sustained effort will lead to more victories here in the future.**
Lastly on Thursday, we’ll be in reliably Blue Dallas. We can’t only show up in just Red places, we have to show up in the Blue ones, too. High turnout in Dallas is critical to winning statewide races. That’s how we beat John Cornyn or Ken Paxton this November.
Showing up everywhere takes resources—paying organizers, supporting volunteers, and putting gas in the tank.
Powered by People shows up to make a difference everywhere. We use every dollar to turn out voters across Texas.
[**If you’re able, I hope you’ll chip in $3 to Powered by People today. It’s your donations that keep us on the road and turning out voters.**](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20260215.kxQB0T&refcode=ema20260215-240691&t=4&akid=240691%2E444211%2ESNsWfm)
**[Donate $3](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20260215.kxQB0T&refcode=ema20260215-240691&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=23&akid=240691%2E444211%2ESNsWfm)**
Thank you,
Beto

[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pbp-em-2023-footer?refcode=em20260215-240691-footer&t=24&refcode2=240691_444211_SNsWfm&akid=240691%2E444211%2ESNsWfm)
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end.
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.betoorourke.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/)
* [Update My Personal Information](https://act.betoorourke.com/signup/update-contact-information/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.betoorourke.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=25&akid=240691%2E444211%2ESNsWfm)

GOP Rep. Steve Scalise **[destroyed Democrat Rep. Nikema Williams'](https://substack.com/redirect/22557199-2c81-47e8-8977-67e3c4e800cd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** half-baked attempt to attack voter ID requirements in the SAVE Act as a Republican "scheme" to "silence voters."
"Over 70% of Democrats support needing a picture ID to vote! 82% of hispanic Americans support a picture ID to vote!" Scalise sounded off, fact checking Williams.
## [Watch the tense moment here: ](https://substack.com/redirect/22557199-2c81-47e8-8977-67e3c4e800cd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

# **AG Bondi releases list of 300 prominent names appearing in Epstein files**
Attorney General Pam Bondi released a list of 300 politicians and prominent figures named in the Epstein files while **[telling Congress that all documents the DOJ was required to reveal have been made public.](https://substack.com/redirect/93e07a99-0150-4a59-b7f5-8fac981a598a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** The list includes President Trump, Barack and Michelle Obama, Bill and Hillary Clinton, Bill Gates, Elon Musk, Jay Z, Kim Kardashian, Prince Harry, Mark Zuckerberg, Kamala Harris, and dozens more -- though inclusion does not imply wrongdoing or direct contact with Epstein.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/93e07a99-0150-4a59-b7f5-8fac981a598a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Why it matters:** A team of hundreds of lawyers combed through some 6 million pages of files and released over 3.5 million pages of material, with privileged materials still being withheld under deliberative-process, work-product, and attorney-client privilege. Bondi and Deputy AG Todd Blanche emphasized that "no records were withheld or redacted on the basis of embarrassment, reputational harm, or political sensitivity, including to any government official, public figure, or foreign dignitary."
* **The big picture:** The release follows controversy over Rep. Ro Khanna publicly naming six men he accused the DOJ of hiding, four of whom turned out to be in a photo lineup with no known ties to Epstein. Deputy AG Blanche fired back on X, saying "you didn't come to us, but immediately ran to X and the House floor and made false accusations about four men, while we were checking the facts." A small number of documents remain in limbo due to ongoing litigation and will be released if a court approves.

* * *
# **Chicago-area teacher forced to resign over "Go ICE" Facebook post speaks out**
James Heidorn, a 14-year veteran physical education teacher at Gary Elementary School in West Chicago, was forced to resign after local activists circulated his Facebook post saying "GO ICE" in support of a local police department cooperating with immigration enforcement. "To see it all upended over two simple words where I expressed my personal support for law enforcement felt like a severe blow to my career," **[Heidorn told Fox News](https://substack.com/redirect/76d33d74-2d92-4b8c-b684-25a37e85d4d3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/2090929f-240c-4a7f-9243-0a502367c0f7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **What happened:** Before any investigation was completed, Illinois Democratic state Sen. Karina Villa publicly condemned the post, and West Chicago Mayor Daniel Bovey posted a Facebook video calling the comments "hurtful" and "offensive." Parents organized a school boycott, and the city held a public "listening session" with a Spanish translator where parents said "kids do not feel safe." Heidorn ultimately resigned a second time rather than face termination, also losing his soccer coaching job at a nearby private school.

* **Why it matters:** Heidorn argues his case represents a dangerous double standard in free speech protections, noting that teachers across the country have stormed businesses and disrupted communities to protest Trump's immigration policies "without facing pushback or repercussions from local school districts." Going forward, Heidorn must disclose to future employers that he resigned and explain why, leaving his teaching career deeply uncertain. "This is bigger than me -- it's about whether personal opinions expressed outside of work can cost someone their livelihood without due process," he said.

* * *
# **Hillary Clinton admits mass migration "went too far" in notable policy shift**
Former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton declared at the Munich Security Conference this week that **[mass migration"went too far, it's been disruptive and destabilizing,](https://substack.com/redirect/8bbecf3c-36a7-4b65-a375-1bc17bda907d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** and it needs to be fixed in a humane way with secure borders that don't torture and kill people" -- a significant departure from her recent emphasis on immigration's economic benefits.
* **Why it matters:** The remarks represent a striking reversal from Clinton's long-standing positions, including her 2016 presidential platform opposing border wall expansion, supporting deferred enforcement for millions of illegal immigrants, and advocating extending healthcare to undocumented families. As recently as last year, she argued immigrants helped the U.S. economy outperform "comparable advanced economies across the world."
* **The big picture:** Clinton's shifting positions on immigration span decades -- from opposing healthcare benefits for undocumented immigrants in 1993, to defending broad protections and expanded government access during her 2016 campaign, to now calling for secure borders. Critics argue her record reveals positions "shaped less by fixed principle than by political fortune and prevailing winds," with each stance closely tracking the political moment rather than a consistent governing philosophy.

clean_body: "The other side is acting like this is the end of the world! 82% of hispanic Americans support needing a picture ID to vote!" | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? "The other side is acting like this is the end of the world! 82% of hispanic Americans support needing a picture ID to vote!" | | | Feb 15 | | | | | | | | | | | | GOP Rep. Steve Scalise half-baked attempt to attack voter ID requirements in the SAVE Act as a Republican "scheme" to "silence voters." "Over 70% of Democrats support needing a picture ID to vote! 82% of hispanic Americans support a picture ID to vote!" Scalise sounded off, fact checking Williams. | | ---|---|--- Did you know some credit cards can actually help you get out of debt faster? Yes, it sounds crazy. But it's true. The secret: Find a card with a "0% intro APR" period for balance transfers. Then, transfer your debt balance and pay it down as much as possible during the intro period. No interest means you could pay off the debt faster. AG Bondi releases list of 300 prominent names appearing in Epstein files Attorney General Pam Bondi released a list of 300 politicians and prominent figures named in the Epstein files while The list includes President Trump, Barack and Michelle Obama, Bill and Hillary Clinton, Bill Gates, Elon Musk, Jay Z, Kim Kardashian, Prince Harry, Mark Zuckerberg, Kamala Harris, and dozens more -- though inclusion does not imply wrongdoing or direct contact with Epstein. | | ---|---|--- Why it matters: A team of hundreds of lawyers combed through some 6 million pages of files and released over 3.5 million pages of material, with privileged materials still being withheld under deliberative-process, work-product, and attorney-client privilege. Bondi and Deputy AG Todd Blanche emphasized that "no records were withheld or redacted on the basis of embarrassment, reputational harm, or political sensitivity, including to any government official, public figure, or foreign dignitary." The big picture: The release follows controversy over Rep. Ro Khanna publicly naming six men he accused the DOJ of hiding, four of whom turned out to be in a photo lineup with no known ties to Epstein. Deputy AG Blanche fired back on X, saying "you didn't come to us, but immediately ran to X and the House floor and made false accusations about four men, while we were checking the facts." A small number of documents remain in limbo due to ongoing litigation and will be released if a court approves. Chicago-area teacher forced to resign over "Go ICE" Facebook post speaks out James Heidorn, a 14-year veteran physical education teacher at Gary Elementary School in West Chicago, was forced to resign after local activists circulated his Facebook post saying "GO ICE" in support of a local police department cooperating with immigration enforcement. "To see it all upended over two simple words where I expressed my personal support for law enforcement felt like a severe blow to my career," . | | ---|---|--- What happened: Before any investigation was completed, Illinois Democratic state Sen. Karina Villa publicly condemned the post, and West Chicago Mayor Daniel Bovey posted a Facebook video calling the comments "hurtful" and "offensive." Parents organized a school boycott, and the city held a public "listening session" with a Spanish translator where parents said "kids do not feel safe." Heidorn ultimately resigned a second time rather than face termination, also losing his soccer coaching job at a nearby private school. Why it matters: Heidorn argues his case represents a dangerous double standard in free speech protections, noting that teachers across the country have stormed businesses and disrupted communities to protest Trump's immigration policies "without facing pushback or repercussions from local school districts." Going forward, Heidorn must disclose to future employers that he resigned and explain why, leaving his teaching career deeply uncertain. "This is bigger than me -- it's about whether personal opinions expressed outside of work can cost someone their livelihood without due process," he said. Hillary Clinton admits mass migration "went too far" in notable policy shift Former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton declared at the Munich Security Conference this week that and it needs to be fixed in a humane way with secure borders that don't torture and kill people" -- a significant departure from her recent emphasis on immigration's economic benefits. Why it matters: The remarks represent a striking reversal from Clinton's long-standing positions, including her 2016 presidential platform opposing border wall expansion, supporting deferred enforcement for millions of illegal immigrants, and advocating extending healthcare to undocumented families. As recently as last year, she argued immigrants helped the U.S. economy outperform "comparable advanced economies across the world." The big picture: Clinton's shifting positions on immigration span decades -- from opposing healthcare benefits for undocumented immigrants in 1993, to defending broad protections and expanded government access during her 2016 campaign, to now calling for secure borders. Critics argue her record reveals positions "shaped less by fixed principle than by political fortune and prevailing winds," with each stance closely tracking the political moment rather than a consistent governing philosophy. * You're currently a free subscriber to . name: Squad Victory Fund
subject: Peter, we need your help today
body:
[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/18910?refcode=em20260215-1940-foote&t=1001&akid=1940%2E342411%2EL0Uq0g ]Squad Victory Fund

Peter,

Our mid-month deadline is tonight — and we won’t sugarcoat it: we need to
close strong.

Trump and the MAGA movement are doing everything they can to silence
progressive voices like ours. They’re scared of what happens when people
organize, speak out, and refuse to back down.

That’s why Alexandria, Ayanna, Ilhan, and Rashida keep showing up — even
when it’s hard, even when the attacks are relentless. They’re fighting for
immigrant families, for working people, for basic dignity and justice.

But they can’t do this alone.

If we’re going to protect their four seats come November while making sure
our champions have the resources they need to keep fighting, we have to
step up — together.

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/19213?c0=svfem-20260215-movement.tIrl_f&refcode=em20260215-1940&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1002&akid=1940%2E342411%2EL0Uq0g ]Peter, if
you’re able, chip in $3 today to help hit our mid-month
goal and be part of the change we’re building together. This movement has
always been powered by people like you. And with everything on the line,
your support matters more than ever.

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/19213?c0=svfem-20260215-movement.tIrl_f&refcode=em20260215-1940&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1021&akid=1940%2E342411%2EL0Uq0g ]Contribute ››

Thank you,

The Squad Victory Fund

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe:

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.squadvictoryfund.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=1940%2E342411%2EL0Uq0g

In addition to what’s legally required, The Squad Victory Fund does not accept contributions from: (1) corporations, associations, or their PACs; (2) individuals registered as federal lobbyists or under FARA; (3) from the PACs, lobbyists, or SEC-named executives of fossil fuel companies; or (4) from the Fraternal Order of Police.

Contributions or gifts to The Squad Victory Fund are not tax deductible.

Contributions to The Squad Victory Fund will be allocated equally among: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Courage to Change, Inspiring Leadership Has a Name PAC, Power of Us PAC, and Rooted in Community Leadership PAC.

Any contributor may designate their contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if any contributor makes a contribution that, when allocated, would exceed the amount that the contributor may lawfully give to any participant.

After contributing to The Squad Victory Fund, you will automatically be signed up to receive email updates from Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, and Rashida Tlaib for Congress. You may of course opt-out by unsubscribing at any time.

name: Stasha Rhodes
subject: We can’t wait.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by United for Democracy
body:
| |
---
Peter, Earlier this week, we shared why we’re preparing now for the Supreme Court’s decision in _Louisiana v. Callais_ , and why waiting until the ruling drops is a mistake. **Since then, one thing has been clear: people**** _want_****to be ready.** They don’t want to wake up to another decision that weakens their voice and have no idea what it means or what to do next.
They don’t want to feel alone while power keeps getting pulled further out of reach. That’s exactly what United for Democracy is here to prevent. Right now, we’re actively preparing the infrastructure people will need when the decision comes down, clear explanations, ways to act together, and plans to show up publicly and visibly in key states. [**But we can’t finish that work without you. Can you chip in to build this movement?**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqsQNarR6PCR1lUtXpWTyhe63nGElJBICfxKS5eORA9eyhe3oXJINU5SF9rjvOxqd5VNypJayk9lbKr152j-5Zl-B2RGGk5BHNCovVoTm5FFJClfPsO7lBIvEaGSLj0Egu8-kj-SWttAeCHn9_zknrAWFYG3nJR_mEjkf7HCdnBHXpOkEjYV_o_TfxuMGNFYYI6rHPM7RGzOATy01qQPczdqLNihkuOg_H1sfDM8SScX2m3SswgELIVWxVYE6x1273fJTtvQ9fhsdDmGSldFBoDS/4o6/M6toNbd1SDOS3d0W3PRTpg/h0/h001.zYLfRw-Dy4KKnFX_HtkNfSGVv1iJLnIYrGrXRzt__YM) **Your contribution helps us:**
• Finalize public-facing tools that explain what the ruling means in plain language
• Equip people with simple ways to act, online and in the streets
• Support rapid, coordinated responses so communities aren’t caught flat-footed
• Make sure this moment doesn’t pass quietly or get buried in confusion This isn’t about one court case. It’s about a political system that keeps shutting people out, and whether we’re ready to push back together. The Supreme Court term is moving quickly. The window to prepare is closing. [**If you believe people deserve a say in decisions that shape their lives, chip in today and help us be ready for what’s coming.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqsQNarR6PCR1lUtXpWTyhe63nGElJBICfxKS5eORA9eyitm3_IIoR4r_ksfaXroO7oLXwqKeMz0cdPH3nLLLiErWLbsumUbTz8CvZaQjxrvLMseqR2khR_w2c5uIqJct8dW5O5tFnE7BOFcvLD-PiLo2U-Go5r_HyJbDpBcY8yelwiWCIeP2gf675_7RNW9W-igS2bMcO31J4cbIKTaR2ZMzEFDNH8IOGd4mZbwV6KyyMrFa1-p--dUwCpuenWiAeH46siXX0n-5nxWIfLJIzvK/4o6/M6toNbd1SDOS3d0W3PRTpg/h1/h001.7bzFeKS5AVAuME4PQdwt4YfAbG9owNOaDy1Zt4gUSm0) Thank you for standing with us,
Stasha Rhodes
United for Democracy P.S. Once the ruling is out, it’s too late to prepare. [**Everything we do now determines how strong our response will be when it matters most.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqsQNarR6PCR1lUtXpWTyhe63nGElJBICfxKS5eORA9eyljnSNyAwVCdV8q408MKRE_KKTx2CP67q_-gSm2xH-zbKGimu-ll6FLPHUpD1RLyEJb4XeNCO3F9CTBIpYG3FZoDXQ5yJ7ipxvWUARNgw6fpHDB9CzAXmDzO-TK-FFVPqshXyjTi7PguONYFUuqua2fzRQrNMPyxxRh1zHjkAIu5vsVVysjxWD7JiSCnAqccFFf0Vco2I8boj5QBw0zrri9KBPLe5gpE33p7_g59zC1z/4o6/M6toNbd1SDOS3d0W3PRTpg/h2/h001.gECU6RS_yae4pVTRUnp8AOZs4zUeMUgGacYOJZAXaZo)

Fundraising has slowed to a crawl — not because Democrats are weak, but because too many Republicans are sitting this moment out.

Meanwhile, Democrats are already **flipping seats** , flooding money into swing districts, and betting that conservative voters won’t show up when it matters most. If they’re right, we lose the House in November. Period.

The margins are razor-thin. Every seat counts. And right now, apathy is doing the Left’s work for them.

I’m fighting every day to hold the line — but I can’t do it alone. If grassroots conservatives don’t step up **now** , the damage will be impossible to undo later.

Please don’t wait. Don’t assume someone else will carry the load.

clean_body: Fundraising has slowed to a crawl — not because Democrats are weak, but because too many Republicans are sitting this moment out. | Fellow American — I’m going to be straight with you. Fundraising has slowed to a crawl — not because Democrats are weak, but because too many Republicans are sitting this moment out. Meanwhile, Democrats are already flipping seats , flooding money into swing districts, and betting that conservative voters won’t show up when it matters most. If they’re right, we lose the House in November. Period. The margins are razor-thin. Every seat counts. And right now, apathy is doing the Left’s work for them. I’m fighting every day to hold the line — but I can’t do it alone. If grassroots conservatives don’t step up now , the damage will be impossible to undo later. Please don’t wait. Don’t assume someone else will carry the load. Thank you for standing with me when it matters most, Beth Van Duyne | | PAID FOR BY BETH VAN DUYNE FOR CONGRESS
