Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

name: Research Team [Voter Action Project]
subject: Steps to increase access to voting?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
Newsweek: How Trans Voters Could Be Excluded From the Election [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA007/1/89215/images/Newsweek_TransgenderVotingBarriers.png]
Nearly 210,000 transgender Americans who were eligible to vote in the 2024 general election faced barriers to voting because of stringent voter ID laws, according to new research.
The study shows that roughly 414,000 voting-eligible transgender Americans lived in the 31 states that primarily conduct their elections in person at the polls and have a voter ID law. Nearly half of transgender voters in those states do not have an ID that correctly reflects their name or gender.
We want to hear from voters with your thoughts. Will you take our quick one-question poll right away?
Should states take steps to improve access to voting for transgender individuals?
By clicking a response below your answer will be automatically recorded:
YES [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-pollresponse?refcode=021526_1yes&amount=15] NO [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-pollresponse?refcode=021526_1no&amount=15]
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
https://www.facebook.com/voteraction [https://www.facebook.com/voteraction] https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org [https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org] https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/ [https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/] https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/ [https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=021526_1&amount=20]
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=021526_1&amount=20]

clean_body: | | | | | Peter, We’re close — $738 close. At this point in the month, that number isn’t abstract. It’s the difference between keeping Chaz’s schedule full and having to make tradeoffs we don’t want to make. It’s the difference between staying on pace with voter outreach this week and pushing things off to “later.” ~~ Chaz is doing what he always does: showing up, taking the calls, going to the events, and talking directly with voters. Our job, as his team, is to make sure the operation behind him keeps up. That’s what this last $738 does. ~~ | | ~~ Thanks for being part of what makes this campaign work. — Team Molder | | ---|---|--- About Chaz Molder Chaz Molder is the Mayor of Columbia, Tennessee, serving since 2019 and re-elected in 2022. A Columbia native and graduate of the University of Tennessee and the University of Memphis Law School, he returned home to build a career in law and public service. As mayor, he founded the Columbia Mayor’s Youth Council to promote civic engagement among young people and emphasizes the importance of being present and responsive to the community. | | | ~~ Click~~ to receive fewer emails. | For years, the state of Massachusetts has ignored their illegal immigrant problems and instituted liberal, politically motivated policies to protect them. Not surprisingly, it hasn’t worked out well for them. **Here in New Hampshire, we’ve done the opposite. We’ve banned sanctuary cities and given law enforcement the tools that they need to secure our northern border and to keep the bad guys out of our state.** The result - New Hampshire is consistently **ranked the #1 safest state** to live and raise a family in the entire country. The last thing New Hampshire’s Governor should be doing is taking advice on safety from the Governor of Massachusetts. **_New York Times_ : Republican Cash Edge Threatens to Swamp Democrats in the Midterms**
Peter, we're going to be real here:

The National Republican Congressional Committee, super PACs, and party leaders like Donald Trump himself have amassed massive war chests ahead of the November election. They have these goals in mind for that money: do whatever it takes to keep their majority in Congress, maintain the status quo, and continue to promote an agenda that serves themselves and leaves everyday Americans behind.

Leaders like Mariannette Miller-Meeks are sure to become a beneficiary to all this cash, but we shouldn't be surprised. **Last cycle, top committee and super PAC groups like this dropped millions of dollars into this race to beat Christina.** Even with all their spending, Christina lost the race by just 799 votes.

With our race being a toss-up and the most competitive in the country, you can guarantee these groups will throw money at Mariannette Miller-Meeks once again. Remember — Mike Johnson has already cut checks for her to the tune of tens of thousands of dollars. Special interests will do whatever it takes to keep this corporate shill in office.

We're preparing for every fight ahead: every ad that will be placed against Christina, every piece of mail that will be printed to tarnish her record, and every dollar that will be spent to keep Mariannette Miller-Meeks representing IA-01. **[But one thing Miller-Meeks will never have is this team, who is committed to supporting real leadership for the people of IA-01 every single day. We don't have to match these war chests dollar for dollar, but we need to be loud enough to prove that momentum is on our side. Will you chip in to show Mariannette Miller-Meeks that her super PACs and allies won't come to her rescue this time?>>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/125462828/601861659/-2011951990?refcode=em26_260215_FRnum_fnd_gq_act___BFT_ettx&utm_campaign=em26_260215_FRnum_fnd_gq_act___BFT_ettx&utm_medium=email&refcode2=8a50cc3f-c10a-f111-832e-0022482a9733&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjIyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4YTUwY2MzZi1jMTBhLWYxMTEtODMyZS0wMDIyNDgyYTk3MzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_FUeIrtmN6-tV79KNhJUqnyYdDV74emCIpj6A1YyZHU=)**

clean_body: | = New York Times : Republican Cash Edge Threatens to Swamp Democrats in the Midterms Peter, we're going to be real here: The National Republican Congressional Committee, super PACs, and party leaders like Donald Trump himself have amassed massive war chests ahead of the November election. They have these goals in mind for that money: do whatever it takes to keep their majority in Congress, maintain the status quo, and continue to promote an agenda that serves themselves and leaves everyday Americans behind. Leaders like Mariannette Miller-Meeks are sure to become a beneficiary to all this cash, but we shouldn't be surprised. Last cycle, top committee and super PAC groups like this dropped millions of dollars into this race to beat Christina. Even with all their spending, Christina lost the race by just 799 votes. With our race being a toss-up and the most competitive in the country, you can guarantee these groups will throw money at Mariannette Miller-Meeks once again. Remember — Mike Johnson has already cut checks for her to the tune of tens of thousands of dollars. Special interests will do whatever it takes to keep this corporate shill in office. We're preparing for every fight ahead: every ad that will be placed against Christina, every piece of mail that will be printed to tarnish her record, and every dollar that will be spent to keep name: Mark Teixeira
subject: Why I'm Running
body:
| | | | | | | | | | | | | | | [|
---](https://teixeiraforcongress.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=aaeca7d0d6f0c241ad9d38cc7&id=e1bac07559&e=2814cf23ed)
| | Peter,
I’m running for Congress because **I’ve seen firsthand the miracle of America** — this truly is **the greatest country the world has ever known.**
My mother was a schoolteacher and my father served in the Navy — they taught me the importance of **hard work, integrity, and above all, my faith in Jesus Christ**.
I started playing baseball at a young age, working hard and training for years — and eventually earned a spot in Major League Baseball, becoming a three-time All-Star and a World Series champion.
Nothing was ever handed to me. I had to earn it, which made it all the more satisfying. That’s the meritocracy we believe in. It’s what America is about. It’s also what Texas is about — **independence, self-reliance, and optimism**.
I’ve been incredibly blessed in my life, both in baseball and in business. Now I want to serve the country that I love.
Most importantly, I want to fight to keep America the greatest country on earth. That means **protecting our freedom** to practice our Christian faith, reversing the crippling debt, securing the border, and making Washington less consequential in our lives.
**America is exceptional** because our Founders believed our rights come from God. They believed in limited government, the rule of law, and sovereignty.
I’m an **outsider, not a career politician** — and in Congress, I’ll fight every day to protect the rights enshrined in our Constitution.
I’m proud to be **endorsed by President Trump and Governor Abbott** , and I’m working hard to earn your support. You can count on me to always put Texas and America first.
Early voting starts **THIS Tuesday, Feb. 17** , and runs through Feb. 27. Or vote on Election Day, March 3.
Thank you, and God bless,
**Mark Teixeira** Trump-Endorsed America First ConservativeTexas’ 21st Congressional District

subject: Arizona is a bellwether
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MARLENE FOR CONGRESS Checks may also be mailed to:
body:
http://secure.actblue.com/donate/mgw_em?refcode=em_header [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/mgw_em?refcode=em_header]

Team,

What happens here is no longer just about Arizona.

It’s a reflection of the national mood and a signal of what’s possible everywhere. That’s why both parties are pouring enormous resources into winning here. They know what we know: what we do in the months leading up to this election could shape outcomes across the country.

If we show our energy, our motivation, and our strength, we can start the blue wave right here.

But that means we have to show up now.

Tonight at midnight, my campaign faces a critical mid-month fundraising deadline. Reaching our goals now impacts the major budget and spending decisions we are making every day, and with our election getting moved two weeks closer, reaching these goals has become even more important.

I'll be honest, friend. I've already told my team to go ahead with aggressive budget plans. In a race like this, we can't afford to be cautious. We need to push ahead, and trust that grassroots supporters like you will step up because you know what's at stake here. That's why I'm asking if you'll chip in $10 or any amount you can afford today. [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/mgw_em?refcode=260215_mgw_bellwether&amount=10]

Give $25 >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/mgw_em?express_lane=true&amount=25&refcode=260215_mgw_bellwether] Give $50 >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/mgw_em?express_lane=true&amount=50&refcode=260215_mgw_bellwether]

If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your secure donation will go through immediately

Every contribution sends a clear message: we’re not waiting for change — we’re building it.

Thank you,

Marlene

About Marlene Galan-Woods
Marlene Galán-Woods is a former broadcast journalist and mother of five who has lived in Arizona for the past 36 years. Her parents fled communist Cuba looking for a better life. Their risk paid off and allowed Marlene to pursue her career as a broadcast journalist which brought her to Arizona. She met the love of her life, Grant Woods, and they raised a family together before he passed away in 2021. Marlene is running for Congress to stand up to extremists and be a voice for all Arizonans. If elected, Marlene will be the first Latina to represent Arizona's 1st district in Congress.

clean_body: [] Team, What happens here is no longer just about Arizona. It’s a reflection of the national mood and a signal of what’s possible everywhere. That’s why both parties are pouring enormous resources into winning here. They know what we know: what we do in the months leading up to this election could shape outcomes across the country. If we show our energy, our motivation, and our strength, we can start the blue wave right here. But that means we have to show up now. Tonight at midnight, my campaign faces a critical mid-month fundraising deadline. Reaching our goals now impacts the major budget and spending decisions we are making every day, and with our election getting moved two weeks closer, reaching these goals has become even more important. I'll be honest, friend. I've already told my team to go ahead with aggressive budget plans. In a race like this, we can't afford to be cautious. We need to push ahead, and trust that grassroots supporters like you will step up because you know what's at stake here. That's why I'm asking if you'll chip in $10 or any amount you can afford today. [] Give $25 >> [] Give $50 >> [] Give $100 >> [] Give $250 >> [] Give $500 >> [] Give other >> [] If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your secure donation will go through immediately Every contribution sends a clear message: we’re not waiting for change — we’re building it. Thank you, Marlene [] About Marlene Galan-Woods Marlene Galán-Woods is a former broadcast journalist and mother of five who has lived in Arizona for the past 36 years. Her parents fled communist Cuba looking for a better life. Their risk paid off and allowed Marlene to pursue her career as a broadcast journalist which brought her to Arizona. She met the love of her life, Grant Woods, and they raised a family together before he passed away in 2021. Marlene is running for Congress to stand up to extremists and be a voice for all Arizonans. If elected, Marlene will be the first Latina to represent Arizona's 1st district in Congress. name: Charles Sauer
subject: American healthcare reacts to crises, rather than prevent them
body:
When patients control resources, healthcare system work for them, rather than around them.
| | | [View in Web Browser](https://link.email.freethinkerdaily.com/click/e129174/VaHR0cHM6Ly9vZGIubnVjbGV1c2VtYWlsLmNvbS9hbXBsaWZ5L3YvTU5FU2g0VWRNdA/SZW1haWxfZnJlZXRoaW5rZXJkYWlseV9jb20yNjAyMTU/LbmNsWjFBY1E2WQ/qP2hpZHM9bmM0WkF1TThwU1FubDBscw/gaZJQvA/scm134cce73)

---
| |
---
**American healthcare reacts to crises, rather than prevent them**
_When patients control resources, healthcare system work for them, rather than around them._
**By** Charles Sauer

* * *

Healthcare is intensely personal, and when devastating news arrives in the exam room, lives and families are changed in an instant. Yet a system bogged down by regulation and bureaucracy too often compounds that pain, forcing patients to navigate the most frightening chapter of their lives alone. We must change that.
That’s why President Trump’s plan to fund patients – not the system – is revolutionary. Shifting power from government bureaucrats, insurance companies, and big hospital systems will allow Americans to make their own healthcare decisions. When patients control the resources, the healthcare system begins to work for them rather than around them.
Today, one of the clearest examples of why this approach matters is Alzheimer’s disease.
In a powerful **[Wall Street Journal op-ed](https://link.email.freethinkerdaily.)** , Dr. Brent Beasley recounts how he was diagnosed with Alzheimer’s at age 57 after losing his job due to unexplained cognitive struggles. Despite being a physician with significant medical knowledge and strong connections, securing a diagnosis took nearly a year – precious time when early intervention can make a life-changing difference.
But Dr. Beasley’s story is not one of despair. It’s one of hope.
Because his disease was detected early enough, his care team successfully intervened. Treatment helped him regain clarity, remain active in his church, and live a purposeful life. His experience is deeply encouraging. You can read his story **[here](https://link.email.freethinkerdaily.)**.
**An Alzheimer’s diagnosis no longer has to mean immediate decline.**
Science is opening doors that did not exist a decade ago. Yet Dr. Beasley’s story demonstrates that America’s healthcare system is largely designed to react to crises rather than prevent them. Patients are often diagnosed too late even though early interventions and lifestyle changes can have profound impacts.
Removing barriers to early testing, expanding access to innovative treatments, and supporting caregivers must become a national priority.
There is another reason policymakers should pay attention.
Analysts, including President Trump’s former Domestic Policy Advisor Joe Grogan and Paragon Research Fellow Ryan Long, increasingly **[warn that Alzheimer’s is not only a medical challenge but an economic one](https://link.email.freethinkerdaily.)**. Without a serious strategy, the growing number of Alzheimer’s cases could strain families, shrink the workforce, and place additional pressure on federal programs.
**China has a plan. Where’s America’s Plan?**
China has launched a national Alzheimer’s effort focused on awareness, screening, and early intervention. America needs a similar focus. The health and wellness of our citizens and the fiscal strength of our nation are at stake.
The encouraging news is that the path forward is becoming clearer. It must include:
* Detecting the disease earlier
* Empowering primary care doctors
* Supporting innovation
* Reducing bureaucratic obstacles
* Putting patients back at the center of care

With the right strategy, America can lead the world by extending not just lifespan, but healthspan, allowing millions to remain active contributors to their families, communities, and economies.
Dr. Beasley reminds us that behind every policy debate is a human story and hope is no longer theoretical. Now the question is whether we will learn from stories like his and act boldly to help families facing an Alzheimer’s diagnosis.

* * *

**About the Author:**
_Charles Sauer is the president of the Market Institute, and the author of Profit Motive: What Drives the Things We Do._

This email was sent to **[dpwillis67@gmail.com](mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com)** as part of your subscription to **Free Thinker Daily**

108 S Washington St. Suite 200
Alexandria, VA 22314

[Unsubscribe](https://link.email.freethinkerdaily.com/click/e129174/VaHR0cHM6Ly9vZGIubnVjbGV1c2VtYWlsLmNvbS9hbXBsaWZ5L3N1YnNjcmlwdGlvbnMvdW5zdWJzY3JpYmU/SZW1haWxfZnJlZXRoaW5rZXJkYWlseV9jb20yNjAyMTU/LbmNsWjFBY1E2WQ/qP2hpZHM9bmM0WkF1TThwU1FubDBscw/gaZJQvA/s3m5432afcd)

Removing barriers to early testing, expanding access to innovative treatments, and supporting caregivers must become a national priority. There is another reason policymakers should pay attention. Analysts, including President Trump’s former Domestic Policy Advisor Joe Grogan and Paragon Research Fellow Ryan Long, increasingly . Without a serious strategy, the growing number of Alzheimer’s cases could strain families, shrink the workforce, and place additional pressure on federal programs. China has a plan. Where’s America’s Plan? China has launched a national Alzheimer’s effort focused on awareness, screening, and early intervention. America needs a similar focus. The health and wellness of our citizens and the fiscal strength of our nation are at stake. The encouraging news is that the path forward is becoming clearer. It must include: Detecting the disease earlier Empowering primary care doctors Supporting innovation Reducing bureaucratic obstacles Putting patients back at the center of care With the right strategy, America can lead the world by extending not just lifespan, but healthspan, allowing millions to remain active contributors to their families, communities, and economies. Dr. Beasley reminds us that behind every policy debate is a human story and hope is no longer theoretical. Now the question is whether we will learn from stories like his and act boldly to help families facing an Alzheimer’s diagnosis. About the Author: Charles Sauer is the president of the Market Institute, and the author of Profit Motive: What Drives the Things We Do.

clean_body: The woke left keeps trying to turn sports into another battleground in the culture war against America and American values. | The woke left keeps trying to turn sports into another battleground in the culture war against America and American values. But some prominent voices are starting to speak up. And NFL legend obliterated woke athletes by dropping this massive truth bomb. Here to Read More. If you or a loved were injured and bleeding out, are you prepared to survive until first responders arrive? If you aren't certain, take a moment to read this sponsored message from our friends at Lone Survivalist: I'm doing something crazy. Selling a life-saving Israeli Bandage for $1. 1 bandage with 6 uses. Paramedics use these in war zones. Works as a tourniquet, compression bandage, sling, and trauma bandage and more... People pay $20 for these. You're getting it for $1. Why? Because I'd rather save your life than make 20 bucks. Don't like it? Email me. Keep it free. Won't last. Lou P.S. One guy said it saved his dog's life 8 years later. name: Charles Sauer
subject: American healthcare reacts to crises, rather than prevent them
body:
When patients control resources, healthcare system work for them, rather than around them.
| | | [View in Web Browser](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e23952981/VaHR0cHM6Ly93cm8ubnVjbGV1c2VtYWlsLmNvbS9hbXBsaWZ5L3YvWFpudVhmREpOcg/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNjAyMTU/LbmNsN3laUzYzNg/qP2hpZHM9bmN6MUJ0V2JXaDV5dlBscw/gaZJVHg/s6of5375ea2)

---
| |
---
**American healthcare reacts to crises, rather than prevent them**
_When patients control resources, healthcare system work for them, rather than around them._
**By** Charles Sauer

* * *

Healthcare is intensely personal, and when devastating news arrives in the exam room, lives and families are changed in an instant. Yet a system bogged down by regulation and bureaucracy too often compounds that pain, forcing patients to navigate the most frightening chapter of their lives alone. We must change that.
That’s why President Trump’s plan to fund patients – not the system – is revolutionary. Shifting power from government bureaucrats, insurance companies, and big hospital systems will allow Americans to make their own healthcare decisions. When patients control the resources, the healthcare system begins to work for them rather than around them.
Today, one of the clearest examples of why this approach matters is Alzheimer’s disease.
In a powerful **[Wall Street Journal op-ed](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)** , Dr. Brent Beasley recounts how he was diagnosed with Alzheimer’s at age 57 after losing his job due to unexplained cognitive struggles. Despite being a physician with significant medical knowledge and strong connections, securing a diagnosis took nearly a year – precious time when early intervention can make a life-changing difference.
But Dr. Beasley’s story is not one of despair. It’s one of hope.
Because his disease was detected early enough, his care team successfully intervened. Treatment helped him regain clarity, remain active in his church, and live a purposeful life. His experience is deeply encouraging. You can read his story **[here](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)**.
**An Alzheimer’s diagnosis no longer has to mean immediate decline.**
Science is opening doors that did not exist a decade ago. Yet Dr. Beasley’s story demonstrates that America’s healthcare system is largely designed to react to crises rather than prevent them. Patients are often diagnosed too late even though early interventions and lifestyle changes can have profound impacts.
Removing barriers to early testing, expanding access to innovative treatments, and supporting caregivers must become a national priority.
There is another reason policymakers should pay attention.
Analysts, including President Trump’s former Domestic Policy Advisor Joe Grogan and Paragon Research Fellow Ryan Long, increasingly **[warn that Alzheimer’s is not only a medical challenge but an economic one](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)**. Without a serious strategy, the growing number of Alzheimer’s cases could strain families, shrink the workforce, and place additional pressure on federal programs.
**China has a plan. Where’s America’s Plan?**
China has launched a national Alzheimer’s effort focused on awareness, screening, and early intervention. America needs a similar focus. The health and wellness of our citizens and the fiscal strength of our nation are at stake.
The encouraging news is that the path forward is becoming clearer. It must include:
* Detecting the disease earlier
* Empowering primary care doctors
* Supporting innovation
* Reducing bureaucratic obstacles
* Putting patients back at the center of care

With the right strategy, America can lead the world by extending not just lifespan, but healthspan, allowing millions to remain active contributors to their families, communities, and economies.
Dr. Beasley reminds us that behind every policy debate is a human story and hope is no longer theoretical. Now the question is whether we will learn from stories like his and act boldly to help families facing an Alzheimer’s diagnosis.

* * *

**About the Author:**
_Charles Sauer is the president of the Market Institute, and the author of Profit Motive: What Drives the Things We Do._

This email was sent to **[dpwillis67@gmail.com](mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com)** as part of your subscription to **America First War Room**

108 S Washington St. Suite 200
Alexandria, VA 22314

[Unsubscribe](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e23952981/VaHR0cHM6Ly93cm8ubnVjbGV1c2VtYWlsLmNvbS9hbXBsaWZ5L3N1YnNjcmlwdGlvbnMvdW5zdWJzY3JpYmU/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNjAyMTU/LbmNsN3laUzYzNg/qP2hpZHM9bmN6MUJ0V2JXaDV5dlBscw/gaZJVHg/s8q11742e34)

body:
| Hey, it’s Jon.

You probably didn’t wake up this morning hoping to read another fundraising email from yet another Democrat. But since you’re here...

**I’m the only Democratic U.S. Senator running for reelection in a state Trump carried in 2024. That makes Georgia MAGA’s top pickup target.**

They’re lining up millions to flip this seat and drown out the truth with disinformation.

I will fight back. And I _will_ win. But I can’t do it alone.

**I really need your help right now.**

[**Please, consider chipping in to my campaign and the Voter Protection Project today. Do it before our mid-month fundraising deadline and help us put up a number that’ll have the GOP shaking in their boots.**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/103383?t=6&akid=23015%2E878179%2EIPz4Qx) If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation between Jon Ossoff and VPP will go through immediately: | [CONTRIBUTE $10 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/103384?t=7&akid=23015%2E878179%2EIPz4Qx)

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=23015%2E878179%2EIPz4Qx). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=23015%2E878179%2EIPz4Qx).**

Team — Donald Trump has called his political opponents “scum” and “enemies of the state.”

And now, he is waging an all-out war on freedom of speech and those who dare to speak out against him.

Trump is coming after Jason for speaking out against his authoritarian rule, and Jason can’t fight back alone. Chip in now to help defend Jason from Trump’s attacks. [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/jc-em-feb26]

He didn't put on the uniform to serve a king — but that's exactly what Donald Trump is acting like.

Can you make a donation now to help Jason keep fighting to build a nation for all of us? He needs you in this fight with him. [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/jc-em-feb26]

name: =?utf-8?q?this_is_your_final_notice=E2=80=A6_Fight_for_Reform?=
subject: wait... you're ignoring us?!?
disclaimer_text: paid for and provided by End Citizens United Nonfederal, P
body:
| Fight for Reform is dedicated to electing Democrats, transforming our broken campaign finance system, and ending _Citizens United_ state by state. If you would like to get fewer emails, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59469?t=1&akid=23930%2E4449941%2EIfeCQl) If you would like to unsubscribe, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59470?t=2&akid=23930%2E4449941%2EIfeCQl) Listen, this is your final notice.

We’ve reached out to you over and over again. It’s probably time to give up, but we had to try and reach you one more time.

All we’re asking for is your signature on our petition to support the Constitutional Amendment to [**END _Citizens United_**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/73053?t=3&akid=23930%2E4449941%2EIfeCQl) before the Supreme Court ruins all of our progress... but you don’t seem to care?!

We thought after we told you how IMPORTANT this was, you would listen — but [**STILL, NOTHING.**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/73053?t=4&akid=23930%2E4449941%2EIfeCQl)

[**The deadline for our petition is TONIGHT at 11:59PM**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/73053?t=5&akid=23930%2E4449941%2EIfeCQl) [**and you are the only Democrat in your area who hasn't responded.**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/73053?t=6&akid=23930%2E4449941%2EIfeCQl)

[ **Will you PLEASE add your name to our petition to support the Constitutional Amendment to end _Citizens United?_ >> ** ](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/73053?t=7&akid=23930%2E4449941%2EIfeCQl) | [SIGN NOW →](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/73053?t=8&akid=23930%2E4449941%2EIfeCQl)
---
Hoping to see your name,

Fight for Reform

From: Horrible Supreme Court news (Fight For Reform)
Subject: Republicans are thrilled...
To: YOU **

[**“Supreme Court agrees to hear Republican-backed effort to lift caps on campaign spending”**

_CNN_ ](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/73053?t=10&akid=23930%2E4449941%2EIfeCQl) | [](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/73053?t=11&akid=23930%2E4449941%2EIfeCQl)

---
WOW – this could change everything in a TERRIBLE WAY.

This case (backed by Vice President J.D. Vance) would allow BILLIONAIRE DONORS to drop TENS of thousands of dollars into party committees without any caps.

The Supreme Court has already ruled in FAVOR of _Citizens United_ and money in politics. So it’s never been so CRUCIAL that we show WIDESPREAD SUPPORT for getting money OUT of politics once and for all.

That’s why Amy Klobuchar is sounding the alarm:
[](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/73053?t=13&akid=23930%2E4449941%2EIfeCQl)
[**So please , before it’s too late: Sign our petition to support the Constitutional Amendment to END _Citizens United_ and stop billionaire donors from rigging our elections →**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/73053?t=14&akid=23930%2E4449941%2EIfeCQl)
[SIGN THE PETITION →](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/73053?t=15&akid=23930%2E4449941%2EIfeCQl)
---
Thanks,

_Fight for Reform_

body:
I need your support.
| | | It's Senator Schumer. This is a long email -- but because of how much is at stake, _you need to know it all._

**If you can't read the whole thing, I'll cut to the chase:**

**[The GOP wants to use their majority to implement ALL of Trump's most extreme policy goals. I wish I was exaggerating, but I'm not. So please, will you chip in $5 _(becomes $15!)_ before tonight's mid-month deadline to take back the Senate?](https://go.dscc.org/go/22916?amount=5&t=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-FD-20260215-ChuckSchumer-SchumerLongForm2026RF-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FD-20260215-ChuckSchumer-SchumerLongForm2026RF-Pros-sl1&akid=22734%2E9716520%2E2QYXZN)**

_Here are the three most important things you need to know:_

**SENATE REPUBLICANS ARE WORKING HAND-IN-HAND WITH TRUMP TO PASS HIS PLANS INTO LAW.**

Now that they have the majority, they're doing whatever it takes to cater to Trump's desires -- even if it means cutting key programs like Medicaid and Social Security!

_It gets worse:_

**EXPERTS ARE SAYING TAKING THE SENATE BACK WILL BE TOUGH.**

If we want to stop Trump's agenda, we'll NEED to take back the Senate -- but the analysts are saying it'll be an uphill battle. Factor in the Republican megadonors willing to spend hundreds of millions to keep the Senate red, and it's clear we won't have much margin for error.

_As a result:_

**WE'LL NEED ALL HANDS ON DECK TO DEFY THE ODDS AND TAKE BACK THE MAJORITY!**

It'll be a tough fight, but I know it can be done. We can only beat the odds if grassroots Democrats like you step up. **So please:**

**[We _NEED_ to fight back and act with urgency, so before today's mid-month deadline, will you give $5 _(becomes $15!)_ to win back the Senate?](https://go.dscc.org/go/22916?amount=5&t=6&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-FD-20260215-ChuckSchumer-SchumerLongForm2026RF-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FD-20260215-ChuckSchumer-SchumerLongForm2026RF-Pros-sl1&akid=22734%2E9716520%2E2QYXZN)**

subject: Fighting for Democracy
body:
District Round-up 02.15.26 Dear Neighbor, Thank you for reading my District Round-up. As your candidate for Pennsylvania’s 13th Congressional District, it is my honor and pleasure to deliver to you...
---
|
---
| | | [Beth Farnham for Congress](https://bethfarnhamforcongress.com) | [Read on blog](https://public-api.wordpress.com/bar/?stat=groovemails-events&bin=wpcom_email_click&redirect_to=https%3A%2F%2Fbethfarnhamforcongress.com%2F2026%2F02%2F15%2Ffighting-for-democracy%2F%3Fjetpack_skip_subscription_popup&sr=0&signature=b8bcb1df9578663deddecc4d8153c454&blog_id=213069660&user=248418217&_&_z=z) or [Reader](https://public-api.wordpress.com/bar/?stat=groovemails-events&bin=wpcom_email_click&redirect_to=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.wordpress.com%2Freader%2Fblogs%2F213069660%2Fposts%2F6338&sr=0&signature=0a69e006e0a1c6787528f05ccfa82a70&blog_id=213069660&user=248418217&_&_z=z)
---|---|---
# [Fighting for Democracy](https://bethfarnhamforcongress.com/2026/02/15/fighting-for-democracy/?jetpack_skip_subscription_popup)
---
By **Elizabeth Farnham** on February 15, 2026
---
| District Round-up 02.15.26
---
Dear Neighbor,
---
Thank you for reading my District Round-up. As your candidate for Pennsylvania’s 13th Congressional District, it is my honor and pleasure to deliver to you where I’ve been and what issues I’ve tackled in the past week. Criss-crossing the counties of Adams, Franklin, Fulton, Bedford, Cambria, Blair, Huntingdon, Mifflin, Juniata, Perry, Cumberland, and Somerset gives me great opportunities to meet with voters, hear what is on your hearts and minds, and the chance to voice my fight for Everyday Americans. If you would like to contribute to my campaign, please donate here: [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/beth-farnham-for-congress-1](http://bethfarnhamforcongress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=b92ebd8ecbaa4f3fd43b6f71320f82a9c882ad9bb7e0444f775c0e387c0852b9&blog_id=213069660&post_id=6338&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=51d40269e4413c1e66586d832db5244c&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_url=aHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUuYWN0Ymx1ZS5jb20vZG9uYXRlL2JldGgtZmFybmhhbS1mb3ItY29uZ3Jlc3MtMQ=&email_id=82e450c1eba1c6f2eca7bfd0b1491d00)
---
Petition season is upon us! If you would like to sign or circulate petitions for Democratic candidates so we can appear on the ballots in Pennsylvania, please reach out to your local Democratic committee. The more signatures for candidates beyond our required totals (mine is 1000), the better! If you don’t know your county committee, please visit this website to find them. [https://www.padems.org/county-parties/](http://bethfarnhamforcongress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=de537bb55085e1a009e3e52913937367facc1b65ea6875f01bdc7ba239b6d669&blog_id=213069660&post_id=6338&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=8b2a193871862e5b76b504f716c8d1c7&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_url=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cucGFkZW1zLm9yZy9jb3VudHktcGFydGllcy8&email_id=82e450c1eba1c6f2eca7bfd0b1491d00)
---
| |
---
Many thanks to all who came out on Saturday to the Love Your Neighbor demonstration hosted by Hanover for Progress.
---
It was a great event of learning more about the candidates and standing up for our neighbors instead of punching them for their heritage at a fraternal club like two Hanoverians did a few weeks ago.
---
Together, we [#ChooseDemocracy](http://bethfarnhamforcongress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=c11fbb057b380c5e9bf62bf63b153ad21f50f6ccbd1dc63d85ec2d40f9a57863&blog_id=213069660&post_id=6338&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=b9fb065d1653894fe1ee838001f43954&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=82e450c1eba1c6f2eca7bfd0b1491d00) and neighborly love!
---
| |
---
On February 11th, John Joyce voted, along with the majority of Republicans, for the SAVE Act which passed the US House of Representatives.
---
This wildly painful, awfully stupid, and deeply un-American legislation basically disenfranchises married women who took their husband’s last name and do not have a passport to prove their citizenship as required to register. Whether these women are registering due to a change of address, name change, change of political party or because they were unjustly removed from voter rolls, the fact is, the SAVE Act will bar them because their names do not match those on their birth certificates which does count as proof of citizenship for those who haven’t changed their names since birth, i.e. men.
---
Except in 5 states of which Pennsylvania is not one, Real ID will not satisfy the proof of citizenship, only a current passport or certain military ID, nor will a marriage certificate which supplies the chain of reason for the name change be permitted in conjunction with a birth certificate and driver’s license, as far as I know. Since passports cost $130, plus more if one is also posing with minor children, this will effectively be a poll tax on women, especially those in rural areas struggling in this economy. Additionally, Republican-controlled states cannot be trusted not to kick off women and other minorities from the voter rolls unjustly causing their need for re-registration in the first place.
---
Lastly, all of this nonsense is due to the false claim that somehow non-citizens were voting. It is, and has been, illegal for non-citizens to vote, but rural married conservative women swallowed that lie hook and sinker.
---
And now their ability to vote, and that of many of other people who need to register for any number of reasons and don’t have a passport, hang in the balance for the US Senate to decide. Because if you can’t register, you can’t vote. And the SAVE Act will take immediate effect if it is signed into law.
---
So if you care about a large swath of voters not losing the right to vote, even if many of them don’t seem to care right now, please call our terrible senators Fetterman and McCormick because they are our last hope.
---
And if married women lose the right to vote, the Republicans in Congress will look for a new group to disenfranchise.
---
Convince our Congress to [#ChooseDemocracy](http://bethfarnhamforcongress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=8e30d533249bc84a3da054494976cdc9ae1db7c58e4fc1a9dd22e2cb89369683&blog_id=213069660&post_id=6338&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=3624288e8ac3eab3821a3f7d38b67840&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=82e450c1eba1c6f2eca7bfd0b1491d00) instead.

If you’re like me, you love this country as much as I do and work to make a positive difference. In the midst of this Constitutional Crisis, please consider reaching out to your local Democratic committees or Democratic candidates so that we can strengthen our Democracy instead of letting it slide into fascism.
---
We don’t just #ChooseDemocracy, we choose Pennsylvania, we choose The United States of America, we choose Humanity and I am deeply glad to work along side you.
---
Sincerely,
---
Beth

As your candidate for Pennsylvania’s 13th Congressional District, it is my honor and pleasure to deliver to you... | | | | | or ---|---|--- By Elizabeth Farnham on February 15, 2026 | District Round-up 02.15.26 Dear Neighbor, Thank you for reading my District Round-up. As your candidate for Pennsylvania’s 13th Congressional District, it is my honor and pleasure to deliver to you where I’ve been and what issues I’ve tackled in the past week. Criss-crossing the counties of Adams, Franklin, Fulton, Bedford, Cambria, Blair, Huntingdon, Mifflin, Juniata, Perry, Cumberland, and Somerset gives me great opportunities to meet with voters, hear what is on your hearts and minds, and the chance to voice my fight for Everyday Americans. If you would like to contribute to my campaign, please donate here: Petition season is upon us! If you would like to sign or circulate petitions for Democratic candidates so we can appear on the ballots in Pennsylvania, please reach out to your local Democratic committee. The more signatures for candidates beyond our required totals (mine is 1000), the better! If you don’t know your county committee, please visit this website to find them. | | Many thanks to all who came out on Saturday to the Love Your Neighbor demonstration hosted by Hanover for Progress. It was a great event of learning more about the candidates and standing up for our neighbors instead of punching them for their heritage at a fraternal club like two Hanoverians did a few weeks ago. Together, we and neighborly love! | | On February 11th, John Joyce voted, along with the majority of Republicans, for the SAVE Act which passed the US House of Representatives. This wildly painful, awfully stupid, and deeply un-American legislation basically disenfranchises married women who took their husband’s last name and do not have a passport to prove their citizenship as required to register. Whether these women are registering due to a change of address, name change, change of political party or because they were unjustly removed from voter rolls, the fact is, the SAVE Act will bar them because their names do not match those on their birth certificates which does count as proof of citizenship for those who haven’t changed their names since birth, i.e. men. Except in 5 states of which Pennsylvania is not one, Real ID will not satisfy the proof of citizenship, only a current passport or certain military ID, nor will a marriage certificate which supplies the chain of reason for the name change be permitted in conjunction with a birth certificate and driver’s license, as far as I know. Since passports cost $130, plus more if one is also posing with minor children, this will effectively be a poll tax on women, especially those in rural areas struggling in this economy. Additionally, Republican-controlled states cannot be trusted not to kick off women and other minorities from the voter rolls unjustly causing their need for re-registration in the first place. Lastly, all of this nonsense is due to the false claim that somehow non-citizens were voting. It is, and has been, illegal for non-citizens to vote, but rural married conservative women swallowed that lie hook and sinker. And now their ability to vote, and that of many of other people who need to register for any number of reasons and don’t have a passport, hang in the balance for the US Senate to decide. Because if you can’t register, you can’t vote. And the SAVE Act will take immediate effect if it is signed into law. So if you care about a large swath of voters not losing the right to vote, even if many of them don’t seem to care right now, please call our terrible senators Fetterman and McCormick because they are our last hope. And if married women lose the right to vote, the Republicans in Congress will look for a new group to disenfranchise. Convince our Congress to instead. | | Many thanks to volunteers Deb Martinez, Angie and Chris Somma, and Evelyn D’Elia for volunteering yesterday and taking photos at the Shippensburg Ice Fest.
